53	class BaseIterable:
54	    def __init__(
55	        self, queryset, chunked_fetch=False, chunk_size=GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE
56	    ):
57	        self.queryset = queryset
58	        self.chunked_fetch = chunked_fetch
59	        self.chunk_size = chunk_size
60	
61	    async def _async_generator(self):
62	        # Generators don't actually start running until the first time you call
63	        # next() on them, so make the generator object in the async thread and
64	        # then repeatedly dispatch to it in a sync thread.
65	        sync_generator = self.__iter__()
66	
67	        def next_slice(gen):
68	            return list(islice(gen, self.chunk_size))
69	
70	        while True:
71	            chunk = await sync_to_async(next_slice)(sync_generator)
72	            for item in chunk:
73	                yield item
74	            if len(chunk) < self.chunk_size:
75	                break
76	
77	    # __aiter__() is a *synchronous* method that has to then return an
78	    # *asynchronous* iterator/generator. Thus, nest an async generator inside
79	    # it.
80	    # This is a generic iterable converter for now, and is going to suffer a
81	    # performance penalty on large sets of items due to the cost of crossing
82	    # over the sync barrier for each chunk. Custom __aiter__() methods should
83	    # be added to each Iterable subclass, but that needs some work in the
84	    # Compiler first.
85	    def __aiter__(self):
86	        return self._async_generator()
87

167	class RawModelIterable(BaseIterable):
168	    """
169	    Iterable that yields a model instance for each row from a raw queryset.
170	    """
171	
172	    def __iter__(self):
173	        # Cache some things for performance reasons outside the loop.
174	        db = self.queryset.db
175	        query = self.queryset.query
176	        connection = connections[db]
177	        compiler = connection.ops.compiler("SQLCompiler")(query, connection, db)
178	        query_iterator = iter(query)
179	
180	        try:
181	            (
182	                model_init_names,
183	                model_init_pos,
184	                annotation_fields,
185	            ) = self.queryset.resolve_model_init_order()
186	            model_cls = self.queryset.model
187	            if any(
188	                f.attname not in model_init_names for f in model_cls._meta.pk_fields
189	            ):
190	                raise exceptions.FieldDoesNotExist(
191	                    "Raw query must include the primary key"
192	                )
193	            fields = [self.queryset.model_fields.get(c) for c in self.queryset.columns]
194	            cols = [f.get_col(f.model._meta.db_table) if f else None for f in fields]
195	            converters = compiler.get_converters(cols)
196	            if converters:
197	                query_iterator = compiler.apply_converters(query_iterator, converters)
198	            if compiler.has_composite_fields(cols):
199	                query_iterator = compiler.composite_fields_to_tuples(
200	                    query_iterator, cols
201	                )
202	            fetch_mode = self.queryset._fetch_mode
203	            peers = []
204	            for values in query_iterator:
205	                # Associate fields to values
206	                model_init_values = [values[pos] for pos in model_init_pos]
207	                instance = model_cls.from_db(
208	                    db, model_init_names, model_init_values, fetch_mode=fetch_mode
209	                )
210	                if fetch_mode.track_peers:
211	                    peers.append(weak_ref(instance))
212	                    instance._state.peers = peers
213	                if annotation_fields:
214	                    for column, pos in annotation_fields:
215	                        setattr(instance, column, values[pos])
216	                yield instance
217	        finally:
218	            # Done iterating the Query. If it has its own cursor, close it.
219	            if hasattr(query, "cursor") and query.cursor:
220	                query.cursor.close()

223	class ValuesIterable(BaseIterable):
224	    """
225	    Iterable returned by QuerySet.values() that yields a dict for each row.
226	    """
227	
228	    def __iter__(self):
229	        queryset = self.queryset
230	        query = queryset.query
231	        compiler = query.get_compiler(queryset.db)
232	
233	        if query.selected:
234	            names = list(query.selected)
235	        else:
236	            # extra(select=...) cols are always at the start of the row.
237	            names = [
238	                *query.extra_select,
239	                *query.values_select,
240	                *query.annotation_select,
241	            ]
242	        indexes = range(len(names))
243	        for row in compiler.results_iter(
244	            chunked_fetch=self.chunked_fetch, chunk_size=self.chunk_size
245	        ):
246	            yield {names[i]: row[i] for i in indexes}
247

249	class ValuesListIterable(BaseIterable):
250	    """
251	    Iterable returned by QuerySet.values_list(flat=False) that yields a tuple
252	    for each row.
253	    """
254	
255	    def __iter__(self):
256	        queryset = self.queryset
257	        query = queryset.query
258	        compiler = query.get_compiler(queryset.db)
259	        return compiler.results_iter(
260	            tuple_expected=True,
261	            chunked_fetch=self.chunked_fetch,
262	            chunk_size=self.chunk_size,
263	        )

266	class NamedValuesListIterable(ValuesListIterable):
267	    """
268	    Iterable returned by QuerySet.values_list(named=True) that yields a
269	    namedtuple for each row.
270	    """
271	
272	    def __iter__(self):
273	        queryset = self.queryset
274	        if queryset._fields:
275	            names = queryset._fields
276	        else:
277	            query = queryset.query
278	            names = [
279	                *query.extra_select,
280	                *query.values_select,
281	                *query.annotation_select,
282	            ]
283	        tuple_class = create_namedtuple_class(*names)
284	        new = tuple.__new__
285	        for row in super().__iter__():
286	            yield new(tuple_class, row)

289	class FlatValuesListIterable(BaseIterable):
290	    """
291	    Iterable returned by QuerySet.values_list(flat=True) that yields single
292	    values.
293	    """

296	        queryset = self.queryset

298	        for row in compiler.results_iter(
299	            chunked_fetch=self.chunked_fetch, chunk_size=self.chunk_size

304	class PreventQuerySetCloning:
305	    """
306	    Temporarily prevent the given QuerySet from creating new QuerySet instances
307	    on each mutating operation (e.g: filter(), exclude() etc), instead
308	    modifying the QuerySet in-place.
309	
310	    @contextlib.contextmanager is intentionally not used for performance
311	    reasons.
312	    """
313	
314	    __slots__ = ("queryset",)
315	
316	    def __init__(self, queryset):
317	        self.queryset = queryset
318	
319	    def __enter__(self):
320	        return self.queryset._disable_cloning()
321	
322	    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback):
323	        self.queryset._enable_cloning()
324

326	class QuerySet(AltersData):
327	    """Represent a lazy database lookup for a set of objects."""
328	
329	    def __init__(self, model=None, query=None, using=None, hints=None):
330	        self.model = model
331	        self._db = using
332	        self._hints = hints or {}
333	        self._query = query or sql.Query(self.model)
334	        self._result_cache = None
335	        self._sticky_filter = False
336	        self._for_write = False
337	        self._prefetch_related_lookups = ()
338	        self._prefetch_done = False
339	        self._known_related_objects = {}  # {rel_field: {pk: rel_obj}}
340	        self._iterable_class = ModelIterable
341	        self._fetch_mode = DEFAULT_FETCH_MODE
342	        self._fields = None
343	        self._defer_next_filter = False
344	        self._deferred_filter = None
345	        self._cloning_enabled = True
346	
347	    @property
348	    def query(self):
349	        if self._deferred_filter:
350	            negate, args, kwargs = self._deferred_filter
351	            self._filter_or_exclude_inplace(negate, args, kwargs)
352	            self._deferred_filter = None
353	        return self._query
354	
355	    @query.setter
356	    def query(self, value):
357	        if value.values_select:
358	            self._iterable_class = ValuesIterable
359	        self._query = value
360	
361	    def as_manager(cls):
362	        # Address the circular dependency between `Queryset` and `Manager`.
363	        from django.db.models.manager import Manager
364	
365	        manager = Manager.from_queryset(cls)()
366	        manager._built_with_as_manager = True
367	        return manager
368	
369	    as_manager.queryset_only = True
370	    as_manager = classmethod(as_manager)
371	
372	    ########################
373	    # PYTHON MAGIC METHODS #
374	    ########################
375	
376	    def __deepcopy__(self, memo):
377	        """Don't populate the QuerySet's cache."""
378	        obj = self.__class__()
379	        for k, v in self.__dict__.items():
380	            if k == "_result_cache":
381	                obj.__dict__[k] = None
382	            else:
383	                obj.__dict__[k] = copy.deepcopy(v, memo)
384	        return obj
385	
386	    def __getstate__(self):
387	        # Force the cache to be fully populated.
388	        self._fetch_all()
389	        return {**self.__dict__, DJANGO_VERSION_PICKLE_KEY: django.__version__}

410	    def __repr__(self):
411	        data = list(self[: REPR_OUTPUT_SIZE + 1])
412	        if len(data) > REPR_OUTPUT_SIZE:
413	            data[-1] = "...(remaining elements truncated)..."
414	        return "<%s %r>" % (self.__class__.__name__, data)

416	    def __len__(self):
417	        self._fetch_all()
418	        return len(self._result_cache)
419	
420	    def __iter__(self):
421	        """
422	        The queryset iterator protocol uses three nested iterators in the
423	        default case:
424	            1. sql.compiler.execute_sql()
425	               - Returns 100 rows at time (constants.GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE)
426	                 using cursor.fetchmany(). This part is responsible for
427	                 doing some column masking, and returning the rows in chunks.
428	            2. sql.compiler.results_iter()
429	               - Returns one row at time. At this point the rows are still just
430	                 tuples. In some cases the return values are converted to
431	                 Python values at this location.
432	            3. self.iterator()
433	               - Responsible for turning the rows into model objects.
434	        """
435	        self._fetch_all()
436	        return iter(self._result_cache)
437	
438	    def __aiter__(self):
439	        # Remember, __aiter__ itself is synchronous, it's the thing it returns
440	        # that is async!
441	        async def generator():
442	            await sync_to_async(self._fetch_all)()
443	            for item in self._result_cache:
444	                yield item
445	
446	        return generator()
447	
448	    def __bool__(self):
449	        self._fetch_all()
450	        return bool(self._result_cache)
451	
452	    def __getitem__(self, k):
453	        """Retrieve an item or slice from the set of results."""
454	        if not isinstance(k, (int, slice)):
455	            raise TypeError(
456	                "QuerySet indices must be integers or slices, not %s."
457	                % type(k).__name__
458	            )
459	        if (isinstance(k, int) and k < 0) or (
460	            isinstance(k, slice)
461	            and (
462	                (k.start is not None and k.start < 0)
463	                or (k.stop is not None and k.stop < 0)
464	            )
465	        ):
466	            raise ValueError("Negative indexing is not supported.")
467	
468	        if self._result_cache is not None:
469	            return self._result_cache[k]
470	
471	        if isinstance(k, slice):
472	            qs = self._chain()
473	            if k.start is not None:
474	                start = int(k.start)
475	            else:
476	                start = None
477	            if k.stop is not None:
478	                stop = int(k.stop)
479	            else:
480	                stop = None
481	            qs.query.set_limits(start, stop)
482	            return list(qs)[:: k.step] if k.step else qs
483	
484	        qs = self._chain()
485	        qs.query.set_limits(k, k + 1)
486	        qs._fetch_all()
487	        return qs._result_cache[0]
488	
489	    def __class_getitem__(cls, *args, **kwargs):
490	        return cls

492	    def __and__(self, other):
493	        self._check_operator_queryset(other, "&")
494	        self._merge_sanity_check(other)

496	            return other
497	        if isinstance(self, EmptyQuerySet):
498	            return self
499	        combined = self._chain()
500	        combined._merge_known_related_objects(other)
501	        combined.query.combine(other.query, sql.AND)
502	        return combined

504	    def __or__(self, other):
505	        self._check_operator_queryset(other, "|")
506	        self._merge_sanity_check(other)
507	        if isinstance(self, EmptyQuerySet):
508	            return other
509	        if isinstance(other, EmptyQuerySet):
510	            return self
511	        query = (
512	            self

546	    def _iterator(self, use_chunked_fetch, chunk_size):
547	        iterable = self._iterable_class(
548	            self,
549	            chunked_fetch=use_chunked_fetch,
550	            chunk_size=chunk_size or 2000,
551	        )
552	        if not self._prefetch_related_lookups or chunk_size is None:
553	            yield from iterable
554	            return
555	
556	        iterator = iter(iterable)
557	        while results := list(islice(iterator, chunk_size)):
558	            prefetch_related_objects(results, *self._prefetch_related_lookups)
559	            yield from results

561	    def iterator(self, chunk_size=None):
562	        """
563	        An iterator over the results from applying this QuerySet to the
564	        database. chunk_size must be provided for QuerySets that prefetch
565	        related objects. Otherwise, a default chunk_size of 2000 is supplied.
566	        """
567	        if chunk_size is None:
568	            if self._prefetch_related_lookups:
569	                raise ValueError(
570	                    "chunk_size must be provided when using QuerySet.iterator() after "
571	                    "prefetch_related()."
572	                )
573	        elif chunk_size <= 0:
574	            raise ValueError("Chunk size must be strictly positive.")
575	        use_chunked_fetch = not connections[self.db].settings_dict.get(
576	            "DISABLE_SERVER_SIDE_CURSORS"
577	        )
578	        return self._iterator(use_chunked_fetch, chunk_size)

580	    async def aiterator(self, chunk_size=None):
581	        """
582	        An asynchronous iterator over the results from applying this QuerySet
583	        to the database.
584	        """
585	        if chunk_size is None:
586	            if self._prefetch_related_lookups:
587	                # RemovedInDjango71Warning: Replace the warning with:
588	                # raise ValueError(
589	                #     "chunk_size must be provided when using "
590	                #     "QuerySet.aiterator() after prefetch_related()."
591	                # )
592	                warnings.warn(
593	                    "Using QuerySet.aiterator() after prefetch_related() without "
594	                    "providing a chunk_size is deprecated and will raise a "
595	                    "ValueError in Django 7.1.",
596	                    category=RemovedInDjango71Warning,
597	                    skip_file_prefixes=django_file_prefixes(),
598	                )
599	                # RemovedInDjango71Warning: When the deprecation ends, remove.
600	                chunk_size = 2000
601	        elif chunk_size <= 0:
602	            raise ValueError("Chunk size must be strictly positive.")
603	        use_chunked_fetch = not connections[self.db].settings_dict.get(
604	            "DISABLE_SERVER_SIDE_CURSORS"
605	        )
606	        iterable = self._iterable_class(
607	            self,
608	            chunked_fetch=use_chunked_fetch,
609	            chunk_size=chunk_size or 2000,
610	        )
611	        if self._prefetch_related_lookups:
612	            results = []
613	
614	            async for item in iterable:
615	                results.append(item)
616	                if len(results) >= chunk_size:
617	                    await aprefetch_related_objects(
618	                        results, *self._prefetch_related_lookups
619	                    )
620	                    for result in results:
621	                        yield result
622	                    results.clear()
623	
624	            if results:
625	                await aprefetch_related_objects(
626	                    results, *self._prefetch_related_lookups
627	                )
628	                for result in results:
629	                    yield result
630	        else:
631	            async for item in iterable:
632	                yield item

634	    def aggregate(self, *args, **kwargs):
635	        """
636	        Return a dictionary containing the calculations (aggregation)
637	        over the current queryset.
638	
639	        If args is present the expression is passed as a kwarg using
640	        the Aggregate object's default alias.
641	        """
642	        if self.query.distinct_fields:
643	            raise NotImplementedError("aggregate() + distinct(fields) not implemented.")
644	        self._validate_values_are_expressions(
645	            (*args, *kwargs.values()), method_name="aggregate"
646	        )
647	        for arg in args:
648	            # The default_alias property raises TypeError if default_alias
649	            # can't be set automatically or AttributeError if it isn't an
650	            # attribute.
651	            try:
652	                arg.default_alias
653	            except (AttributeError, TypeError):
654	                raise TypeError("Complex aggregates require an alias")
655	            kwargs[arg.default_alias] = arg
656	
657	        return self.query.chain().get_aggregation(self.db, kwargs)
658	
659	    async def aaggregate(self, *args, **kwargs):
660	        return await sync_to_async(self.aggregate)(*args, **kwargs)

662	    def count(self):
663	        """
664	        Perform a SELECT COUNT() and return the number of records as an
665	        integer.
666	
667	        If the QuerySet is already fully cached, return the length of the
668	        cached results set to avoid multiple SELECT COUNT(*) calls.
669	        """
670	        if self._result_cache is not None:
671	            return len(self._result_cache)
672	
673	        return self.query.get_count(using=self.db)
674	
675	    async def acount(self):

678	    def get(self, *args, **kwargs):
679	        """
680	        Perform the query and return a single object matching the given
681	        keyword arguments.
682	        """
683	        if self.query.combinator and (args or kwargs):
684	            raise NotSupportedError(
685	                "Calling QuerySet.get(...) with filters after %s() is not "
686	                "supported." % self.query.combinator
687	            )
688	        clone = self._chain() if self.query.combinator else self.filter(*args, **kwargs)
689	        if self.query.can_filter() and not self.query.distinct_fields:
690	            clone = clone.order_by()
691	        limit = None
692	        if (
693	            not clone.query.select_for_update
694	            or connections[clone.db].features.supports_select_for_update_with_limit
695	        ):
696	            limit = MAX_GET_RESULTS
697	            clone.query.set_limits(high=limit)
698	        num = len(clone)
699	        if num == 1:
700	            return clone._result_cache[0]
701	        if not num:
702	            raise self.model.DoesNotExist(
703	                "%s matching query does not exist." % self.model._meta.object_name
704	            )
705	        raise self.model.MultipleObjectsReturned(
706	            "get() returned more than one %s -- it returned %s!"
707	            % (
708	                self.model._meta.object_name,
709	                num if not limit or num < limit else "more than %s" % (limit - 1),
710	            )
711	        )
712	
713	    async def aget(self, *args, **kwargs):
714	        return await sync_to_async(self.get)(*args, **kwargs)
715	
716	    def create(self, **kwargs):
717	        """
718	        Create a new object with the given kwargs, saving it to the database
719	        and returning the created object.
720	        """
721	        reverse_one_to_one_fields = frozenset(kwargs).intersection(
722	            self.model._meta._reverse_one_to_one_field_names
723	        )
724	        if reverse_one_to_one_fields:
725	            raise ValueError(
726	                "The following fields do not exist in this model: %s"
727	                % ", ".join(reverse_one_to_one_fields)
728	            )
729	
730	        obj = self.model(**kwargs)
731	        self._for_write = True
732	        obj.save(force_insert=True, using=self.db)
733	        obj._state.fetch_mode = self._fetch_mode
734	        return obj

743	    def _prepare_for_bulk_create(self, objs):
744	        objs_with_pk, objs_without_pk = [], []
745	        for obj in objs:
746	            if isinstance(obj.pk, DatabaseDefault):
747	                objs_without_pk.append(obj)
748	            elif obj._is_pk_set():
749	                objs_with_pk.append(obj)
750	            else:
751	                obj.pk = obj._meta.pk.get_pk_value_on_save(obj)
752	                if obj._is_pk_set():
753	                    objs_with_pk.append(obj)
754	                else:
755	                    objs_without_pk.append(obj)
756	            obj._prepare_related_fields_for_save(operation_name="bulk_create")
757	        return objs_with_pk, objs_without_pk

759	    def _check_bulk_create_options(
760	        self, ignore_conflicts, update_conflicts, update_fields, unique_fields
761	    ):
762	        if ignore_conflicts and update_conflicts:
763	            raise ValueError(
764	                "ignore_conflicts and update_conflicts are mutually exclusive."
765	            )
766	        db_features = connections[self.db].features
767	        if ignore_conflicts:
768	            if not db_features.supports_ignore_conflicts:
769	                raise NotSupportedError(
770	                    "This database backend does not support ignoring conflicts."
771	                )
772	            return OnConflict.IGNORE
773	        elif update_conflicts:
774	            if not db_features.supports_update_conflicts:
775	                raise NotSupportedError(
776	                    "This database backend does not support updating conflicts."
777	                )
778	            if not update_fields:
779	                raise ValueError(
780	                    "Fields that will be updated when a row insertion fails "
781	                    "on conflicts must be provided."
782	                )
783	            if unique_fields and not db_features.supports_update_conflicts_with_target:
784	                raise NotSupportedError(
785	                    "This database backend does not support updating "
786	                    "conflicts with specifying unique fields that can trigger "
787	                    "the upsert."
788	                )
789	            if not unique_fields and db_features.supports_update_conflicts_with_target:
790	                raise ValueError(
791	                    "Unique fields that can trigger the upsert must be provided."
792	                )
793	            # Updating primary keys and non-concrete fields is forbidden.
794	            if any(not f.concrete for f in update_fields):
795	                raise ValueError(
796	                    "bulk_create() can only be used with concrete fields in "
797	                    "update_fields."
798	                )
799	            if any(f in self.model._meta.pk_fields for f in update_fields):
800	                raise ValueError(
801	                    "bulk_create() cannot be used with primary keys in "
802	                    "update_fields."
803	                )
804	            if unique_fields:
805	                if any(not f.concrete for f in unique_fields):
806	                    raise ValueError(
807	                        "bulk_create() can only be used with concrete fields "
808	                        "in unique_fields."
809	                    )
810	            return OnConflict.UPDATE
811	        return None
812	
813	    def bulk_create(
814	        self,
815	        objs,
816	        batch_size=None,
817	        ignore_conflicts=False,
818	        update_conflicts=False,
819	        update_fields=None,
820	        unique_fields=None,
821	    ):
822	        """
823	        Insert each of the instances into the database. Do *not* call
824	        save() on each of the instances, do not send any pre/post_save
825	        signals, and do not set the primary key attribute if it is an
826	        autoincrement field (except if
827	        features.can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert=True).
828	        Multi-table models are not supported.
829	        """
830	        # When you bulk insert you don't get the primary keys back (if it's an
831	        # autoincrement, except if can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert=True), so
832	        # you can't insert into the child tables which references this. There
833	        # are two workarounds:
834	        # 1) This could be implemented if you didn't have an autoincrement pk
835	        # 2) You could do it by doing O(n) normal inserts into the parent
836	        #    tables to get the primary keys back and then doing a single bulk
837	        #    insert into the childmost table.
838	        # We currently set the primary keys on the objects when using
839	        # PostgreSQL via the RETURNING ID clause. It should be possible for
840	        # Oracle as well, but the semantics for extracting the primary keys is
841	        # trickier so it's not done yet.
842	        if batch_size is not None and batch_size <= 0:
843	            raise ValueError("Batch size must be a positive integer.")
844	        # Check that the parents share the same concrete model with the our
845	        # model to detect the inheritance pattern ConcreteGrandParent ->
846	        # MultiTableParent -> ProxyChild. Simply checking
847	        # self.model._meta.proxy would not identify that case as involving
848	        # multiple tables.
849	        for parent in self.model._meta.all_parents:
850	            if parent._meta.concrete_model is not self.model._meta.concrete_model:
851	                raise ValueError("Can't bulk create a multi-table inherited model")
852	        if not objs:
853	            return objs
854	        opts = self.model._meta
855	        if unique_fields:
856	            # Primary key is allowed in unique_fields.
857	            unique_fields = [
858	                self.model._meta.get_field(opts.pk.name if name == "pk" else name)
859	                for name in unique_fields
860	            ]
861	        if update_fields:
862	            update_fields = [self.model._meta.get_field(name) for name in update_fields]
863	        on_conflict = self._check_bulk_create_options(
864	            ignore_conflicts,
865	            update_conflicts,
866	            update_fields,
867	            unique_fields,
868	        )
869	        self._for_write = True
870	        fields = [f for f in opts.concrete_fields if not f.generated]
871	        objs = list(objs)
872	        objs_with_pk, objs_without_pk = self._prepare_for_bulk_create(objs)
873	        if objs_with_pk and objs_without_pk:
874	            context = transaction.atomic(using=self.db, savepoint=False)
875	        else:
876	            context = nullcontext()
877	        with context:
878	            self._handle_order_with_respect_to(objs)
879	            if objs_with_pk:
880	                returned_columns = self._batched_insert(
881	                    objs_with_pk,
882	                    fields,
883	                    batch_size,
884	                    on_conflict=on_conflict,
885	                    update_fields=update_fields,
886	                    unique_fields=unique_fields,
887	                )
888	                for obj_with_pk, results in zip(objs_with_pk, returned_columns):
889	                    for result, field in zip(results, opts.db_returning_fields):
890	                        setattr(obj_with_pk, field.attname, result)
891	                for obj_with_pk in objs_with_pk:
892	                    obj_with_pk._state.adding = False
893	                    obj_with_pk._state.db = self.db

1227	    def in_bulk(self, id_list=None, *, field_name="pk"):
1228	        """
1229	        Return a dictionary mapping each of the given IDs to the object with
1230	        that ID. If `id_list` isn't provided, evaluate the entire QuerySet.
1231	        """
1232	        if self.query.is_sliced:
1233	            raise TypeError("Cannot use 'limit' or 'offset' with in_bulk().")
1234	        if id_list is not None and not id_list:
1235	            return {}
1236	        opts = self.model._meta
1237	        unique_fields = [
1238	            constraint.fields[0]
1239	            for constraint in opts.total_unique_constraints
1240	            if len(constraint.fields) == 1
1241	        ]
1242	        if (
1243	            field_name != "pk"
1244	            and not opts.get_field(field_name).unique
1245	            and field_name not in unique_fields
1246	            and self.query.distinct_fields != (field_name,)
1247	        ):
1248	            raise ValueError(
1249	                "in_bulk()'s field_name must be a unique field but %r isn't."
1250	                % field_name
1251	            )
1252	
1253	        qs = self
1254	
1255	        def get_obj(obj):
1256	            return obj
1257	
1258	        if issubclass(self._iterable_class, ModelIterable):
1259	            # Raise an AttributeError if field_name is deferred.
1260	            get_key = operator.attrgetter(field_name)
1261	
1262	        elif issubclass(self._iterable_class, ValuesIterable):
1263	            if field_name not in self.query.values_select:
1264	                qs = qs.values(field_name, *self.query.values_select)
1265	
1266	                def get_obj(obj):  # noqa: F811
1267	                    # We can safely mutate the dictionaries returned by
1268	                    # ValuesIterable here, since they are limited to the scope
1269	                    # of this function, and get_key runs before get_obj.
1270	                    del obj[field_name]
1271	                    return obj
1272	
1273	            get_key = operator.itemgetter(field_name)
1274	
1275	        elif issubclass(self._iterable_class, ValuesListIterable):
1276	            try:
1277	                field_index = self.query.values_select.index(field_name)
1278	            except ValueError:
1279	                # field_name is missing from values_select, so add it.
1280	                field_index = 0
1281	                if issubclass(self._iterable_class, NamedValuesListIterable):
1282	                    kwargs = {"named": True}
1283	                else:
1284	                    kwargs = {}
1285	                    get_obj = operator.itemgetter(slice(1, None))
1286	                qs = qs.values_list(field_name, *self.query.values_select, **kwargs)
1287	
1288	            get_key = operator.itemgetter(field_index)
1289	
1290	        elif issubclass(self._iterable_class, FlatValuesListIterable):
1291	            if self.query.values_select == (field_name,):
1292	                # Mapping field_name to itself.
1293	                get_key = get_obj
1294	            else:
1295	                # Transform it back into a non-flat values_list().
1296	                qs = qs.values_list(field_name, *self.query.values_select)
1297	                get_key = operator.itemgetter(0)
1298	                get_obj = operator.itemgetter(1)
1299	
1300	        else:
1301	            raise TypeError(
1302	                f"in_bulk() cannot be used with {self._iterable_class.__name__}."
1303	            )
1304	
1305	        if id_list is not None:
1306	            filter_key = "{}__in".format(field_name)
1307	            id_list = tuple(id_list)
1308	            batch_size = connections[self.db].ops.bulk_batch_size([opts.pk], id_list)
1309	            # If the database has a limit on the number of query parameters
1310	            # (e.g. SQLite), retrieve objects in batches if necessary.
1311	            if batch_size and batch_size < len(id_list):
1312	                results = ()
1313	                for offset in range(0, len(id_list), batch_size):
1314	                    batch = id_list[offset : offset + batch_size]
1315	                    results += tuple(qs.filter(**{filter_key: batch}))
1316	                qs = results
1317	            else:
1318	                qs = qs.filter(**{filter_key: id_list})
1319	        else:
1320	            qs = qs._chain()
1321	        return {get_key(obj): get_obj(obj) for obj in qs}

1327	        )

1329	    def delete(self):
1330	        """Delete the records in the current QuerySet."""
1331	        self._not_support_combined_queries("delete")
1332	        if self.query.is_sliced:
1333	            raise TypeError("Cannot use 'limit' or 'offset' with delete().")
1334	        if self.query.distinct_fields:
1335	            raise TypeError("Cannot call delete() after .distinct(*fields).")
1336	        if self._fields is not None:
1337	            raise TypeError("Cannot call delete() after .values() or .values_list()")
1338	
1339	        del_query = self._chain()
1340	
1341	        # The delete is actually 2 queries - one to find related objects,
1342	        # and one to delete. Make sure that the discovery of related
1343	        # objects is performed on the same database as the deletion.
1344	        del_query._for_write = True
1345	
1346	        # Disable non-supported fields.
1347	        del_query.query.select_for_update = False
1348	        del_query.query.select_related = False
1349	        del_query.query.clear_ordering(force=True)
1350	
1351	        collector = Collector(using=del_query.db, origin=self)
1352	        collector.collect(del_query)
1353	        num_deleted, num_deleted_per_model = collector.delete()
1354	
1355	        # Clear the result cache, in case this QuerySet gets reused.
1356	        self._result_cache = None
1357	        return num_deleted, num_deleted_per_model

1369	        """
1370	        Delete objects found from the given queryset in single direct SQL
1371	        query. No signals are sent and there is no protection for cascades.
1372	        """
1373	        query = self.query.clone()
1374	        query.__class__ = sql.DeleteQuery
1375	        return query.get_compiler(using).execute_sql(ROW_COUNT)

1379	    def update(self, **kwargs):
1380	        """
1381	        Update all elements in the current QuerySet, setting all the given
1382	        fields to the appropriate values.
1383	        """
1384	        self._not_support_combined_queries("update")
1385	        if self.query.is_sliced:
1386	            raise TypeError("Cannot update a query once a slice has been taken.")
1387	        if self.query.distinct_fields:
1388	            raise TypeError("Cannot call update() after .distinct(*fields).")
1389	        self._for_write = True
1390	        query = self.query.chain(sql.UpdateQuery)
1391	        query.add_update_values(kwargs)
1392

2007	    def ordered(self):
2008	        """
2009	        Return True if the QuerySet is ordered -- i.e. has an order_by()
2010	        clause or a default ordering on the model (or is empty).
2011	        """
2012	        if isinstance(self, EmptyQuerySet):
2013	            return True
2014	        if self.query.extra_order_by or self.query.order_by:
2015	            return True
2016	        elif (
2017	            self.query.default_ordering
2018	            and self.query.get_meta().ordering
2019	            and
2020	            # A default ordering doesn't affect GROUP BY queries.
2021	            not self.query.group_by
2022	        ):

2024	        else:

2027	    @property
2028	    def totally_ordered(self):
2029	        """
2030	        Returns True if the QuerySet is ordered and the ordering is
2031	        deterministic. This requires that the ordering includes a field
2032	        (or set of fields) that is unique and non-nullable.
2033	
2034	        For queries involving a GROUP BY clause, the model's default
2035	        ordering is ignored. Ordering specified via .extra(order_by=...)
2036	        is also ignored.
2037	        """
2038	        if not self.ordered:
2039	            return False
2040	        ordering = self.query.order_by
2041	        if not ordering and self.query.default_ordering:
2042	            ordering = self.query.get_meta().ordering
2043	        if not ordering:
2044	            return False
2045	        opts = self.model._meta
2046	        pk_fields = {f.attname for f in opts.pk_fields}
2047	        ordering_fields = set()
2048	        for part in ordering:
2049	            # Search for single field providing a total ordering.
2050	            field_name = None
2051	            if isinstance(part, str):
2052	                field_name = part.lstrip("-")
2053	            elif isinstance(part, F):
2054	                field_name = part.name
2055	            elif isinstance(part, OrderBy) and isinstance(part.expression, F):
2056	                field_name = part.expression.name
2057	            if field_name:
2058	                if field_name == "pk":
2059	                    return True
2060	                # Normalize attname references by using get_field().
2061	                try:
2062	                    field = opts.get_field(field_name)
2063	                except exceptions.FieldDoesNotExist:
2064	                    # Could be "?" for random ordering or a related field
2065	                    # lookup. Skip this part of introspection for now.
2066	                    continue
2067	                # Ordering by a related field name orders by the referenced
2068	                # model's ordering. Skip this part of introspection for now.
2069	                if field.remote_field and field_name == field.name:
2070	                    continue
2071	                if field.attname in pk_fields and len(pk_fields) == 1:
2072	                    return True
2073	                if field.unique and not field.null:
2074	                    return True
2075	                ordering_fields.add(field.attname)
2076	
2077	        # Account for members of a CompositePrimaryKey.
2078	        if ordering_fields.issuperset(pk_fields):
2079	            return True
2080	        # No single total ordering field, try unique_together and total
2081	        # unique constraints.
2082	        constraint_field_names = (
2083	            *opts.unique_together,
2084	            *(constraint.fields for constraint in opts.total_unique_constraints),
2085	        )
2086	        for field_names in constraint_field_names:
2087	            # Normalize attname references by using get_field().
2088	            try:
2089	                fields = [opts.get_field(field_name) for field_name in field_names]
2090	            except exceptions.FieldDoesNotExist:
2091	                continue
2092	            # Composite unique constraints containing a nullable column
2093	            # cannot ensure total ordering.
2094	            if any(field.null for field in fields):
2095	                continue
2096	            if ordering_fields.issuperset(field.attname for field in fields):
2097	                return True
2098	
2099	        return False

156	class RawQuery:
157	    """A single raw SQL query."""
158	
159	    def __init__(self, sql, using, params=()):
160	        self.params = params
161	        self.sql = sql
162	        self.using = using
163	        self.cursor = None
164	
165	        # Mirror some properties of a normal query so that
166	        # the compiler can be used to process results.
167	        self.low_mark, self.high_mark = 0, None  # Used for offset/limit
168	        self.extra_select = {}
169	        self.annotation_select = {}
170	
171	    def chain(self, using):
172	        return self.clone(using)
173	
174	    def clone(self, using):
175	        return RawQuery(self.sql, using, params=self.params)
176	
177	    def get_columns(self):
178	        if self.cursor is None:
179	            self._execute_query()
180	        converter = connections[self.using].introspection.identifier_converter
181	        return [converter(column_meta[0]) for column_meta in self.cursor.description]
182	
183	    def __iter__(self):
184	        # Always execute a new query for a new iterator.
185	        # This could be optimized with a cache at the expense of RAM.
186	        self._execute_query()
187	        if not connections[self.using].features.can_use_chunked_reads:
188	            # If the database can't use chunked reads we need to make sure we
189	            # evaluate the entire query up front.
190	            result = list(self.cursor)
191	        else:
192	            result = self.cursor
193	        return iter(result)
194	
195	    def __repr__(self):
196	        return "<%s: %s>" % (self.__class__.__name__, self)
197	
198	    @property
199	    def params_type(self):
200	        if self.params is None:
201	            return None
202	        return dict if isinstance(self.params, Mapping) else tuple
203	
204	    def __str__(self):
205	        if self.params_type is None:
206	            return self.sql
207	        return self.sql % self.params_type(self.params)
208	
209	    def _execute_query(self):
210	        connection = connections[self.using]
211	
212	        # Adapt parameters to the database, as much as possible considering
213	        # that the target type isn't known. See #17755.
214	        params_type = self.params_type
215	        adapter = connection.ops.adapt_unknown_value
216	        if params_type is tuple:
217	            params = tuple(adapter(val) for val in self.params)
218	        elif params_type is dict:
219	            params = {key: adapter(val) for key, val in self.params.items()}
220	        elif params_type is None:
221	            params = None
222	        else:
223	            raise RuntimeError("Unexpected params type: %s" % params_type)
224	
225	        self.cursor = connection.cursor()
226	        self.cursor.execute(self.sql, params)

276	    # Arbitrary limit for select_related to prevents infinite recursion.

740	        # enough, too.

461	        """
462	        if not aggregate_exprs:

464	        # Store annotation mask prior to temporarily adding aggregations for
465	        # resolving purpose to facilitate their subsequent removal.
466	        refs_subquery = False
467	        refs_window = False
468	        replacements = {}
469	        annotation_select_mask = self.annotation_select_mask
470	        for alias, aggregate_expr in aggregate_exprs.items():

474	            )

483	                for ref in aggregate_refs

485	            refs_window |= any(

487	                for ref in aggregate_refs
488	            )
489	            aggregate = aggregate.replace_expressions(replacements)

491	            replacements[Ref(alias, aggregate)] = aggregate
492	        # Stash resolved aggregates now that they have been allowed to resolve
493	        # against each other.

495	        self.set_annotation_mask(annotation_select_mask)

500	            any(

502	                for annotation in self.annotations.values()
503	            )
504	            or having
505	        )
506	        set_returning_annotations = {
507	            alias
508	            for alias, annotation in self.annotation_select.items()
509	            if getattr(annotation, "set_returning", False)
510	        }
511	        # Decide if we need to use a subquery.
512	        #
513	        # Existing aggregations would cause incorrect results as
514	        # get_aggregation() must produce just one result and thus must not use
515	        # GROUP BY.
516	        #
517	        # If the query has limit or distinct, or uses set operations, then
518	        # those operations must be done in a subquery so that the query
519	        # aggregates on the limit and/or distinct results instead of applying
520	        # the distinct and limit after the aggregation.
521	        if (
522	            isinstance(self.group_by, tuple)
523	            or self.is_sliced
524	            or has_existing_aggregation
525	            or refs_subquery
526	            or refs_window
527	            or qualify
528	            or self.distinct
529	            or self.combinator
530	            or set_returning_annotations

388	    def clone(self):
389	        """
390	        Return a copy of the current Query. A lightweight alternative to
391	        deepcopy().
392	        """
393	        obj = Empty()
394	        obj.__class__ = self.__class__
395	        # Copy references to everything.
396	        obj.__dict__ = self.__dict__.copy()
397	        # Clone attributes that can't use shallow copy.
398	        obj.alias_refcount = self.alias_refcount.copy()
399	        obj.alias_map = self.alias_map.copy()
400	        obj.external_aliases = self.external_aliases.copy()
401	        obj.table_map = self.table_map.copy()
402	        obj.where = self.where.clone()
403	        obj.annotations = self.annotations.copy()
404	        if self.annotation_select_mask is not None:
405	            obj.annotation_select_mask = self.annotation_select_mask.copy()
406	        if self.combined_queries:
407	            obj.combined_queries = tuple(
408	                [query.clone() for query in self.combined_queries]
409	            )
410	        # _annotation_select_cache cannot be copied, as doing so breaks the
411	        # (necessary) state in which both annotations and
412	        # _annotation_select_cache point to the same underlying objects.
413	        # It will get re-populated in the cloned queryset the next time it's
414	        # used.
415	        obj._annotation_select_cache = None
416	        obj.extra = self.extra.copy()
417	        if self.extra_select_mask is not None:
418	            obj.extra_select_mask = self.extra_select_mask.copy()
419	        if self._extra_select_cache is not None:
420	            obj._extra_select_cache = self._extra_select_cache.copy()
421	        if self.select_related is not False:
422	            # Use deepcopy because select_related stores fields in nested
423	            # dicts.
424	            obj.select_related = copy.deepcopy(obj.select_related)
425	        if "subq_aliases" in self.__dict__:
426	            obj.subq_aliases = self.subq_aliases.copy()
427	        obj.used_aliases = self.used_aliases.copy()
428	        obj._filtered_relations = self._filtered_relations.copy()
429	        # Clear the cached_property, if it exists.
430	        obj.__dict__.pop("base_table", None)
431	        return obj

433	    def chain(self, klass=None):
434	        """
435	        Return a copy of the current Query that's ready for another operation.
436	        The klass argument changes the type of the Query, e.g. UpdateQuery.
437	        """
438	        obj = self.clone()
439	        if klass and obj.__class__ != klass:
440	            obj.__class__ = klass
441	        if not obj.filter_is_sticky:
442	            obj.used_aliases = set()
443	        obj.filter_is_sticky = False
444	        if hasattr(obj, "_setup_query"):
445	            obj._setup_query()
446	        return obj

1488	    def build_filter(

1506	        negations. This will be used to determine if subqueries are needed.

1510	        is needed.
1511

1513	        branch_negated is set on first negation, but current_negated is
1514	        flipped for each negation.

1517	        upper in the code by add_q().

1520

1522	        query. However, if the filter isn't added to the query then the caller

1446	        def _get_field_choices():
1447	            """Yield all allowed field paths in breadth-first search order."""
1448	            queue = collections.deque([(None, self.klass_info)])
1449	            while queue:

1451	                if parent_path is None:

1454	                else:

1461	                    (path, klass_info)

1463	                )

1514	    def get_converters(self, expressions):
1515	        i = 0
1516	        converters = {}
1517	
1518	        for expression in expressions:

1520	                cols = expression.get_source_expressions()
1521	                cols_converters = self.get_converters(cols)
1522	                for j, (convs, col) in cols_converters.items():
1523	                    converters[i + j] = (convs, col)
1524	                i += len(expression)

1527	                field_converters = expression.get_db_converters(self.connection)
1528	                if backend_converters or field_converters:
1529	                    converters[i] = (backend_converters + field_converters, expression)
1530	                i += 1
1531	            else:
1532	                i += 1

1536	    def apply_converters(self, rows, converters):
1537	        connection = self.connection
1538	        converters = list(converters.items())
1539	        for row in map(list, rows):
1540	            for pos, (convs, expression) in converters:
1541	                value = row[pos]

1555	            for i, expression in enumerate(expressions)

1560	            for pos in col_pair_slices:

1603	        - MULTI: Retrieves all rows using fetchmany(). Wraps in an iterator for
1604	           chunked reads when supported.
1605	        - SINGLE: Retrieves a single row using fetchone().
1606	        - ROW_COUNT: Retrieves the number of rows in the result.
1607	        - CURSOR: Runs the query, and returns the cursor object. It is the
1608	           caller's responsibility to close the cursor.
1609	        """
1610	        result_type = result_type or NO_RESULTS
1611	        try:
1612	            sql, params = self.as_sql()
1613	            if not sql:
1614	                raise EmptyResultSet
1615	        except EmptyResultSet:
1616	            if result_type == MULTI:
1617	                return iter([])
1618	            else:
1619	                return
1620	        if chunked_fetch:
1621	            cursor = self.connection.chunked_cursor()
1622	        else:
1623	            cursor = self.connection.cursor()
1624	        try:
1625	            cursor.execute(sql, params)
1626	        except Exception as e:
1627	            # Might fail for server-side cursors (e.g. connection closed)
1628	            try:
1629	                cursor.close()
1630	            except DatabaseError:
1631	                raise e from None
1632	            raise
1633	
1634	        if result_type == ROW_COUNT:
1635	            try:
1636	                return cursor.rowcount
1637	            finally:
1638	                cursor.close()
1639	        if result_type == CURSOR:
1640	            # Give the caller the cursor to process and close.
1641	            return cursor
1642	        if result_type == SINGLE:
1643	            try:
1644	                val = cursor.fetchone()
1645	                if val:
1646	                    return val[0 : self.col_count]
1647	                return val
1648	            finally:
1649	                # done with the cursor
1650	                cursor.close()
1651	        if result_type == NO_RESULTS:
1652	            cursor.close()
1653	            return
1654	
1655	        result = cursor_iter(
1656	            cursor,
1657	            self.connection.features.empty_fetchmany_value,
1658	            self.col_count if self.has_extra_select else None,
1659	            chunk_size,
1660	        )
1661	        if not chunked_fetch or not self.connection.features.can_use_chunked_reads:
1662	            # If we are using non-chunked reads, we return the same data
1663	            # structure as normally, but ensure it is all read into memory
1664	            # before going any further. Use chunked_fetch if requested,
1665	            # unless the database doesn't support it.
1666	            return list(result)
1667	        return result

1669	    def explain_query(self):
1670	        result = list(self.execute_sql())
1671	        # Some backends return 1 item tuples with strings, and others return
1672	        # tuples with integers and strings. Flatten them out into strings.
1673	        format_ = self.query.explain_info.format
1674	        output_formatter = json.dumps if format_ and format_.lower() == "json" else str
1675	        for row in result:
1676	            for value in row:
1677	                if not isinstance(value, str):
1678	                    yield " ".join([output_formatter(c) for c in value])
1679	                else:
1680	                    yield value

1683	class SQLInsertCompiler(SQLCompiler):
1684	    returning_fields = None
1685	    returning_params = ()
1686	
1687	    def field_as_sql(self, field, get_placeholder_sql, val):
1688	        """
1689	        Take a field and a value intended to be saved on that field, and
1690	        return placeholder SQL and accompanying params. Check for raw values,
1691	        fields with get_placeholder_sql(), and compilable defined in that
1692	        order.
1693	
1694	        When field is None, consider the value raw and use it as the
1695	        placeholder, with no corresponding parameters returned.
1696	        """
1697	        if field is None:
1698	            # A field value of None means the value is raw.
1699	            sql, params = val, []
1700	        elif get_placeholder_sql is not None:
1701	            # Some fields (e.g. geo fields) need special munging before
1702	            # they can be inserted.
1703	            sql, params = get_placeholder_sql(val, self, self.connection)
1704	        elif hasattr(val, "as_sql"):
1705	            # This is an expression, let's compile it.
1706	            sql, params = self.compile(val)
1707	        else:
1708	            # Return the common case for the placeholder
1709	            sql, params = "%s", [val]
1710	
1711	        # The following hook is only used by Oracle Spatial, which sometimes
1712	        # needs to yield 'NULL' and () as its placeholder and params instead
1713	        # of '%s' and (None,). The 'NULL' placeholder is produced earlier by
1714	        # OracleOperations.get_geom_placeholder(). The following line removes
1715	        # the corresponding None parameter. See ticket #10888.
1716	        params = self.connection.ops.modify_insert_params(sql, params)
1717	
1718	        return sql, params
1719	
1720	    def prepare_value(self, field, value):
1721	        """
1722	        Prepare a value to be used in a query by resolving it if it is an
1723	        expression and otherwise calling the field's get_db_prep_save().
1724	        """
1725	        if hasattr(value, "resolve_expression"):
1726	            value = value.resolve_expression(
1727	                self.query, allow_joins=False, for_save=True
1728	            )
1729	            # Don't allow values containing Col expressions. They refer to
1730	            # existing columns on a row, but in the case of insert the row
1731	            # doesn't exist yet.
1732	            if value.contains_column_references:
1733	                raise ValueError(
1734	                    'Failed to insert expression "%s" on %s. F() expressions '
1735	                    "can only be used to update, not to insert." % (value, field)
1736	                )
1737	            if value.contains_aggregate:
1738	                raise FieldError(
1739	                    "Aggregate functions are not allowed in this query "
1740	                    "(%s=%r)." % (field.name, value)
1741	                )
1742	            if value.contains_over_clause:
1743	                raise FieldError(
1744	                    "Window expressions are not allowed in this query (%s=%r)."
1745	                    % (field.name, value)
1746	                )
1747	        return field.get_db_prep_save(value, connection=self.connection)
1748	
1749	    def pre_save_val(self, field, obj):
1750	        """
1751	        Get the given field's value off the given obj. pre_save() is used for
1752	        things like auto_now on DateTimeField. Skip it if this is a raw query.
1753	        """
1754	        if self.query.raw:
1755	            return getattr(obj, field.attname)
1756	        return field.pre_save(obj, add=True)
1757	
1758	    def assemble_as_sql(self, fields, value_rows):
1759	        """
1760	        Take a sequence of N fields and a sequence of M rows of values, and
1761	        generate placeholder SQL and parameters for each field and value.
1762	        Return a pair containing:
1763	         * a sequence of M rows of N SQL placeholder strings, and
1764	         * a sequence of M rows of corresponding parameter values.
1765	
1766	        Each placeholder string may contain any number of '%s' interpolation
1767	        strings, and each parameter row will contain exactly as many params
1768	        as the total number of '%s's in the corresponding placeholder row.
1769	        """
1770	        if not value_rows:
1771	            return [], []
1772	
1773	        # list of (sql, [params]) tuples for each object to be saved
1774	        # Shape: [n_objs][n_fields][2]
1775	        get_placeholder_sqls = [
1776	            getattr(field, "get_placeholder_sql", None) for field in fields
1777	        ]
1778	        rows_of_fields_as_sql = (
1779	            (
1780	                self.field_as_sql(field, get_placeholder_sql, value)
1781	                for field, get_placeholder_sql, value in zip(
1782	                    fields, get_placeholder_sqls, row
1783	                )
1784	            )
1785	            for row in value_rows
1786	        )
1787	
1788	        # tuple like ([sqls], [[params]s]) for each object to be saved
1789	        # Shape: [n_objs][2][n_fields]
1790	        sql_and_param_pair_rows = (zip(*row) for row in rows_of_fields_as_sql)
1791	
1792	        # Extract separate lists for placeholders and params.
1793	        # Each of these has shape [n_objs][n_fields]
1794	        placeholder_rows, param_rows = zip(*sql_and_param_pair_rows)

339	    def _order_by_pairs(self):
340	        if self.query.extra_order_by:
341	            ordering = self.query.extra_order_by
342	        elif not self.query.default_ordering:
343	            ordering = self.query.order_by
344	        elif self.query.order_by:
345	            ordering = self.query.order_by
346	        elif (meta := self.query.get_meta()) and meta.ordering:
347	            ordering = meta.ordering
348	            self._meta_ordering = ordering
349	        else:
350	            ordering = []
351	        if self.query.standard_ordering:
352	            default_order, _ = ORDER_DIR["ASC"]
353	        else:
354	            default_order, _ = ORDER_DIR["DESC"]
355	
356	        selected_exprs = {}
357	        # Avoid computing `selected_exprs` if there is no `ordering` as it's
358	        # relatively expensive.
359	        if ordering and (select := self.select):
360	            for ordinal, (expr, _, alias) in enumerate(select, start=1):
361	                pos_expr = PositionRef(ordinal, alias, expr)
362	                if alias:
363	                    selected_exprs[alias] = pos_expr
364	                selected_exprs[expr] = pos_expr
365	
366	        for field in ordering:
367	            if hasattr(field, "resolve_expression"):
368	                if isinstance(field, Value):
369	                    # output_field must be resolved for constants.
370	                    field = Cast(field, field.output_field)
371	                if not isinstance(field, OrderBy):
372	                    field = field.asc()
373	                if not self.query.standard_ordering:
374	                    field = field.copy()
375	                    field.reverse_ordering()
376	                select_ref = selected_exprs.get(field.expression)
377	                if select_ref or (
378	                    isinstance(field.expression, F)
379	                    and (select_ref := selected_exprs.get(field.expression.name))
380	                ):
381	                    # Emulation of NULLS (FIRST|LAST) cannot be combined with
382	                    # the usage of ordering by position.
383	                    if (
384	                        field.nulls_first is None and field.nulls_last is None
385	                    ) or self.connection.features.supports_order_by_nulls_modifier:
386	                        field = field.copy()
387	                        field.expression = select_ref
388	                    # Alias collisions are not possible when dealing with
389	                    # combined queries so fallback to it if emulation of NULLS
390	                    # handling is required.
391	                    elif self.query.combinator:
392	                        field = field.copy()
393	                        field.expression = Ref(select_ref.refs, select_ref.source)
394	                yield field, select_ref is not None
395	                continue
396	            if field == "?":  # random
397	                yield OrderBy(Random()), False
398	                continue
399	
400	            col, order = get_order_dir(field, default_order)
401	            descending = order == "DESC"
402	
403	            if select_ref := selected_exprs.get(col):
404	                # Reference to expression in SELECT clause
405	                yield (
406	                    OrderBy(
407	                        select_ref,
408	                        descending=descending,
409	                    ),
410	                    True,
411	                )
412	                continue
413	
414	            if expr := self.query.annotations.get(col):
415	                ref = col
416	                transforms = []
417	            else:
418	                ref, *transforms = col.split(LOOKUP_SEP)
419	                expr = self.query.annotations.get(ref)
420	            if expr:
421	                if self.query.combinator and self.select:
422	                    if transforms:
423	                        raise NotImplementedError(
424	                            "Ordering combined queries by transforms is not "
425	                            "implemented."
426	                        )
427	                    # Don't use the resolved annotation because other
428	                    # combined queries might define it differently.
429	                    expr = F(ref)
430	                if transforms:
431	                    for name in transforms:
432	                        expr = self.query.try_transform(expr, name)
433	                if isinstance(expr, Value):
434	                    # output_field must be resolved for constants.
435	                    expr = Cast(expr, expr.output_field)
436	                yield OrderBy(expr, descending=descending), False
437	                continue
438	
439	            if "." in field and field in self.query.extra_order_by:
440	                # This came in through an extra(order_by=...) addition. Pass it
441	                # on verbatim.
442	                table, col = col.split(".", 1)
443	                yield (
444	                    OrderBy(
445	                        RawSQL("%s.%s" % (self.quote_name(table), col), []),
446	                        descending=descending,
447	                    ),
448	                    False,
449	                )
450	                continue
451	
452	            if self.query.extra and col in self.query.extra:
453	                if col in self.query.extra_select:
454	                    yield (
455	                        OrderBy(
456	                            Ref(col, RawSQL(*self.query.extra[col])),
457	                            descending=descending,
458	                        ),
459	                        True,
460	                    )
461	                else:
462	                    yield (
463	                        OrderBy(RawSQL(*self.query.extra[col]), descending=descending),
464	                        False,
465	                    )
466	            else:
467	                if self.query.combinator and self.select:
468	                    # Don't use the first model's field because other
469	                    # combinated queries might define it differently.
470	                    yield OrderBy(F(col), descending=descending), False
471	                else:
472	                    # 'col' is of the form 'field' or 'field1__field2' or
473	                    # '-field1__field2__field', etc.
474	                    yield from self.find_ordering_name(
475	                        field,
476	                        self.query.get_meta(),
477	                        default_order=default_order,
478	                    )

480	    def get_order_by(self):
481	        """
482	        Return a list of 2-tuples of the form (expr, (sql, params, is_ref)) for
483	        the ORDER BY clause.
484	
485	        The order_by clause can alter the select clause (for example it can add
486	        aliases to clauses that do not yet have one, or it can add totally new
487	        select clauses).
488	        """
489	        result = []
490	        seen = set()
491	        for expr, is_ref in self._order_by_pairs():
492	            resolved = expr.resolve_expression(self.query, allow_joins=True, reuse=None)
493	            if not is_ref and self.query.combinator and self.select:
494	                src = resolved.expression
495	                expr_src = expr.expression
496	                for sel_expr, _, col_alias in self.select:
497	                    if src == sel_expr:
498	                        # When values() is used the exact alias must be used to
499	                        # reference annotations.
500	                        if (
501	                            self.query.has_select_fields
502	                            and col_alias in self.query.annotation_select
503	                            and not (
504	                                isinstance(expr_src, F) and col_alias == expr_src.name
505	                            )
506	                        ):
507	                            continue
508	                        resolved.set_source_expressions(
509	                            [Ref(col_alias if col_alias else src.target.column, src)]
510	                        )
511	                        break
512	                else:
513	                    # Add column used in ORDER BY clause to the selected
514	                    # columns and to each combined query.
515	                    order_by_idx = len(self.query.select) + 1
516	                    col_alias = f"__orderbycol{order_by_idx}"
517	                    for q in self.query.combined_queries:
518	                        # If fields were explicitly selected through values()
519	                        # combined queries cannot be augmented.
520	                        if q.has_select_fields:
521	                            raise DatabaseError(
522	                                "ORDER BY term does not match any column in "
523	                                "the result set."
524	                            )
525	                        q.add_annotation(expr_src, col_alias)
526	                    self.query.add_select_col(resolved, col_alias)
527	                    resolved.set_source_expressions([Ref(col_alias, src)])
528	            sql, params = self.compile(resolved)
529	            # Don't add the same column twice, but the order direction is
530	            # not taken into account so we strip it. When this entire method
531	            # is refactored into expressions, then we can check each part as we
532	            # generate it.
533	            without_ordering = self.ordering_parts.search(sql)[1]
534	            params_hash = make_hashable(params)
535	            if (without_ordering, params_hash) in seen:
536	                continue
537	            seen.add((without_ordering, params_hash))
538	            result.append((resolved, (sql, params, is_ref)))
539	        return result

541	    def get_extra_select(self, order_by, select):
542	        extra_select = []
543	        if self.query.distinct and not self.query.distinct_fields:
544	            select_sql = [t[1] for t in select]
545	            for expr, (sql, params, is_ref) in order_by:
546	                without_ordering = self.ordering_parts.search(sql)[1]
547	                if not is_ref and (without_ordering, params) not in select_sql:
548	                    extra_select.append((expr, (without_ordering, params), None))
549	        return extra_select

202	    def collapse_group_by(self, expressions, having):
203	        # If the database supports group by functional dependence reduction,
204	        # then the expressions can be reduced to the set of selected table
205	        # primary keys as all other columns are functionally dependent on them.
206	        if self.connection.features.allows_group_by_selected_pks:
207	            # Filter out all expressions associated with a table's primary key
208	            # present in the grouped columns. This is done by identifying all
209	            # tables that have their primary key included in the grouped
210	            # columns and removing non-primary key columns referring to them.
211	            # Unmanaged models are excluded because they could be representing
212	            # database views on which the optimization might not be allowed.
213	            pks = {
214	                expr
215	                for expr in expressions
216	                if (
217	                    hasattr(expr, "target")
218	                    and expr.target.primary_key
219	                    and self.connection.features.allows_group_by_selected_pks_on_model(
220	                        expr.target.model
221	                    )
222	                )
223	            }
224	            aliases = {expr.alias for expr in pks}
225	            expressions = [
226	                expr
227	                for expr in expressions
228	                if expr in pks
229	                or expr in having
230	                or getattr(expr, "alias", None) not in aliases
231	            ]
232	        return expressions

241	    def get_select(self, with_col_aliases=False):
242	        """
243	        Return three values:
244	        - a list of 3-tuples of (expression, (sql, params), alias)
245	        - a klass_info structure,
246	        - a dictionary of annotations
247	
248	        The (sql, params) is what the expression will produce, and alias is the
249	        "AS alias" for the column (possibly None).
250	
251	        The klass_info structure contains the following information:
252	        - The base model of the query.
253	        - Which columns for that model are present in the query (by
254	          position of the select clause).
255	        - related_klass_infos: [f, klass_info] to descent into
256	
257	        The annotations is a dictionary of {'attname': column position} values.
258	        """
259	        select = []
260	        klass_info = None
261	        annotations = {}
262	        assert not (self.query.select and self.query.default_cols)
263	        select_mask = self.query.get_select_mask()
264	        if self.query.default_cols:
265	            cols = self.get_default_columns(select_mask)
266	        else:
267	            # self.query.select is a special case. These columns never go to
268	            # any model.
269	            cols = self.query.select
270	        selected = []
271	        select_fields = None
272	        if self.query.selected is None:
273	            selected = [
274	                *(
275	                    (alias, RawSQL(*args))
276	                    for alias, args in self.query.extra_select.items()
277	                ),
278	                *((None, col) for col in cols),
279	                *self.query.annotation_select.items(),
280	            ]
281	            select_fields = list(
282	                range(
283	                    len(self.query.extra_select),
284	                    len(self.query.extra_select) + len(cols),
285	                )
286	            )
287	        else:
288	            select_fields = []
289	            for index, (alias, expression) in enumerate(self.query.selected.items()):
290	                # Reference to an annotation.
291	                if isinstance(expression, str):
292	                    expression = self.query.annotations[expression]
293	                # Reference to a column.
294	                elif isinstance(expression, int):
295	                    select_fields.append(index)
296	                    expression = cols[expression]
297	                # ColPairs cannot be aliased.
298	                if isinstance(expression, ColPairs):
299	                    alias = None
300	                selected.append((alias, expression))
301	        if select_fields:
302	            klass_info = {"model": self.query.model, "select_fields": select_fields}
303	
304	        for select_idx, (alias, expression) in enumerate(selected):
305	            if alias:
306	                annotations[alias] = select_idx
307	            select.append((expression, alias))
308	
309	        if self.query.select_related:
310	            related_klass_infos = self.get_related_selections(select, select_mask)
311	            klass_info["related_klass_infos"] = related_klass_infos
312	
313	            self.get_select_from_parent(klass_info)
314	
315	        ret = []
316	        col_idx = 1
317	        for col, alias in select:
318	            try:
319	                sql, params = self.compile(col)
320	            except EmptyResultSet:
321	                empty_result_set_value = getattr(
322	                    col, "empty_result_set_value", NotImplemented
323	                )
324	                if empty_result_set_value is NotImplemented:
325	                    # Select a predicate that's always False.
326	                    sql, params = "0", ()
327	                else:
328	                    sql, params = self.compile(Value(empty_result_set_value))
329	            except FullResultSet:
330	                sql, params = self.compile(Value(True))
331	            else:
332	                sql, params = col.select_format(self, sql, params)
333	            if alias is None and with_col_aliases:
334	                alias = f"col{col_idx}"
335	                col_idx += 1
336	            ret.append((col, (sql, params), alias))
337	        return ret, klass_info, annotations

1141	    def get_from_clause(self):
1142	        """
1143	        Return a list of strings that are joined together to go after the
1144	        "FROM" part of the query, as well as a list any extra parameters that

1168	            if (
1169	                alias not in self.query.alias_map
1170	                or self.query.alias_refcount[alias] == 1
1171	            ):
1172	                result.append(", %s" % self.quote_name(alias))
