100	        """
101	        Return a list of 2-tuples of form (sql, params).
102	
103	        The logic of what exactly the GROUP BY clause contains is hard
104	        to describe in other words than "if it passes the test suite,
105	        then it is correct".
106	        """
107	        # Some examples:
108	        #     SomeModel.objects.annotate(Count('somecol'))
109	        #     GROUP BY: all fields of the model
110	        #
111	        #    SomeModel.objects.values('name').annotate(Count('somecol'))
112	        #    GROUP BY: name
113	        #
114	        #    SomeModel.objects.annotate(Count('somecol')).values('name')
115	        #    GROUP BY: all cols of the model
116	        #
117	        #    SomeModel.objects.values('name', 'pk')
118	        #    .annotate(Count('somecol')).values('pk')
119	        #    GROUP BY: name, pk
120	        #
121	        #    SomeModel.objects.values('name').annotate(Count('somecol')).values('pk')
122	        #    GROUP BY: name, pk
123	        #
124	        # In fact, the self.query.group_by is the minimal set to GROUP BY. It
125	        # can't be ever restricted to a smaller set, but additional columns in
126	        # HAVING, ORDER BY, and SELECT clauses are added to it. Unfortunately
127	        # the end result is that it is impossible to force the query to have
128	        # a chosen GROUP BY clause - you can almost do this by using the form:
129	        #     .values(*wanted_cols).annotate(AnAggregate())
130	        # but any later annotations, extra selects, values calls that
131	        # refer some column outside of the wanted_cols, order_by, or even
132	        # filter calls can alter the GROUP BY clause.
133	
134	        # The query.group_by is either None (no GROUP BY at all), True
135	        # (group by select fields), or a list of expressions to be added
136	        # to the group by.
137	        if self.query.group_by is None:
138	            return []
139	        expressions = []
140	        group_by_refs = set()
141	        if self.query.group_by is not True:
142	            # If the group by is set to a list (by .values() call most likely),
143	            # then we need to add everything in it to the GROUP BY clause.
144	            # Backwards compatibility hack for setting query.group_by. Remove
145	            # when we have public API way of forcing the GROUP BY clause.
146	            # Converts string references to expressions.
147	            for expr in self.query.group_by:
148	                if not hasattr(expr, "as_sql"):
149	                    expr = self.query.resolve_ref(expr)
150	                if isinstance(expr, Ref):
151	                    if expr.refs not in group_by_refs:
152	                        group_by_refs.add(expr.refs)
153	                        expressions.append(expr.source)
154	                else:
155	                    expressions.append(expr)
156	        # Note that even if the group_by is set, it is only the minimal
157	        # set to group by. So, we need to add cols in select, order_by, and
158	        # having into the select in any case.
159	        selected_expr_positions = {}
160	        for ordinal, (expr, _, alias) in enumerate(select, start=1):
161	            if alias:
162	                selected_expr_positions[expr] = ordinal
163	            # Skip members of the select clause that are already explicitly
164	            # grouped against.
165	            if alias in group_by_refs:
166	                continue
167	            expressions.extend(expr.get_group_by_cols())
168	        if not self._meta_ordering:
169	            for expr, (sql, params, is_ref) in order_by:
170	                # Skip references to the SELECT clause, as all expressions in
171	                # the SELECT clause are already part of the GROUP BY.
172	                if not is_ref:
173	                    expressions.extend(expr.get_group_by_cols())
174	        having_group_by = self.having.get_group_by_cols() if self.having else ()
175	        for expr in having_group_by:
176	            expressions.append(expr)
177	        result = []
178	        seen = set()
179	        expressions = self.collapse_group_by(expressions, having_group_by)
180	
181	        allows_group_by_select_index = (
182	            self.connection.features.allows_group_by_select_index
183	        )
184	        for expr in expressions:
185	            try:
186	                sql, params = self.compile(expr)
187	            except (EmptyResultSet, FullResultSet):
188	                continue
189	            if (
190	                allows_group_by_select_index
191	                and (position := selected_expr_positions.get(expr)) is not None
192	            ):
193	                sql, params = str(position), ()
194	            else:
195	                sql, params = expr.select_format(self, sql, params)
196	            params_hash = make_hashable(params)
197	            if (sql, params_hash) not in seen:
198	                result.append((sql, params))
199	                seen.add((sql, params_hash))

575	        vendor_impl = getattr(node, "as_" + self.connection.vendor, None)
576	        if vendor_impl:
577	            sql, params = vendor_impl(self, self.connection)

582	    def get_combinator_sql(self, combinator, all):
583	        features = self.connection.features
584	        compilers = [
585	            query.get_compiler(self.using, self.connection, self.elide_empty)
586	            for query in self.query.combined_queries
587	        ]
588	        if not features.supports_slicing_ordering_in_compound:
589	            for compiler in compilers:
590	                if compiler.query.is_sliced:
591	                    raise DatabaseError(
592	                        "LIMIT/OFFSET not allowed in subqueries of compound statements."
593	                    )
594	                if compiler.get_order_by():
595	                    raise DatabaseError(
596	                        "ORDER BY not allowed in subqueries of compound statements."
597	                    )
598	        parts = []
599	        empty_compiler = None
600	        for compiler in compilers:
601	            try:
602	                parts.append(self._get_combinator_part_sql(compiler))
603	            except EmptyResultSet:
604	                # Omit the empty queryset with UNION and with DIFFERENCE if the
605	                # first queryset is nonempty.
606	                if combinator == "union" or (combinator == "difference" and parts):
607	                    empty_compiler = compiler
608	                    continue
609	                raise
610	        if not parts:
611	            raise EmptyResultSet
612	        elif len(parts) == 1 and combinator == "union" and self.query.is_sliced:
613	            # A sliced union cannot be composed of a single component because
614	            # in the event the later is also sliced it might result in invalid
615	            # SQL due to the usage of multiple LIMIT clauses. Prevent that from
616	            # happening by always including an empty resultset query to force
617	            # the creation of an union.
618	            empty_compiler.elide_empty = False
619	            parts.append(self._get_combinator_part_sql(empty_compiler))
620	        combinator_sql = self.connection.ops.set_operators[combinator]
621	        if all and combinator == "union":
622	            combinator_sql += " ALL"
623	        braces = "{}"
624	        if not self.query.subquery and features.supports_slicing_ordering_in_compound:
625	            braces = "({})"
626	        sql_parts, args_parts = zip(
627	            *((braces.format(sql), args) for sql, args in parts)
628	        )
629	        result = [" {} ".format(combinator_sql).join(sql_parts)]
630	        params = []
631	        for part in args_parts:
632	            params.extend(part)
633	        return result, params
634	
635	    def _get_combinator_part_sql(self, compiler):
636	        features = self.connection.features
637	        # If the columns list is limited, then all combined queries
638	        # must have the same columns list. Set the selects defined on
639	        # the query on all combined queries, if not already set.
640	        selected = self.query.selected
641	        if selected is not None and compiler.query.selected is None:
642	            compiler.query = compiler.query.clone()
643	            compiler.query.set_values(selected)
644	        part_sql, part_args = compiler.as_sql(with_col_aliases=True)
645	        if compiler.query.combinator:
646	            # Wrap in a subquery if wrapping in parentheses isn't
647	            # supported.
648	            if not features.supports_parentheses_in_compound:
649	                part_sql = "SELECT * FROM ({})".format(part_sql)
650	            # Add parentheses when combining with compound query if not
651	            # already added for all compound queries.
652	            elif (
653	                self.query.subquery
654	                or not features.supports_slicing_ordering_in_compound
655	            ):
656	                part_sql = "({})".format(part_sql)
657	        elif self.query.subquery and features.supports_slicing_ordering_in_compound:
658	            part_sql = "({})".format(part_sql)
659	        return part_sql, part_args
660	
661	    def get_qualify_sql(self):
662	        where_parts = []
663	        if self.where:
664	            where_parts.append(self.where)
665	        if self.having:
666	            where_parts.append(self.having)
667	        inner_query = self.query.clone()
668	        inner_query.subquery = True
669	        inner_query.where = inner_query.where.__class__(where_parts)
670	        # Augment the inner query with any window function references that
671	        # might have been masked via values() and alias(). If any masked
672	        # aliases are added they'll be masked again to avoid fetching
673	        # the data in the `if qual_aliases` branch below.
674	        select = {
675	            expr: alias for expr, _, alias in self.get_select(with_col_aliases=True)[0]
676	        }
677	        select_aliases = set(select.values())
678	        qual_aliases = set()
679	        replacements = {}
680	
681	        def collect_replacements(expressions):
682	            while expressions:
683	                expr = expressions.pop()
684	                if expr in replacements:
685	                    continue
686	                elif select_alias := select.get(expr):
687	                    replacements[expr] = select_alias
688	                elif isinstance(expr, Lookup):
689	                    expressions.extend(expr.get_source_expressions())
690	                elif isinstance(expr, Ref):
691	                    if expr.refs not in select_aliases:
692	                        expressions.extend(expr.get_source_expressions())
693	                else:
694	                    num_qual_alias = len(qual_aliases)
695	                    select_alias = f"qual{num_qual_alias}"
696	                    qual_aliases.add(select_alias)
697	                    inner_query.add_annotation(expr, select_alias)
698	                    replacements[expr] = select_alias

50	    def __init__(self, query, connection, using, elide_empty=True):
51	        self.query = query
52	        self.connection = connection
53	        self.using = using
54	        # Some queries, e.g. coalesced aggregation, need to be executed even if
55	        # they would return an empty result set.
56	        self.elide_empty = elide_empty
57	        self.quote_cache = {"*": "*"}
58	        # The select, klass_info, and annotations are needed by
59	        # QuerySet.iterator() these are set as a side-effect of executing the
60	        # query. Note that we calculate separately a list of extra select
61	        # columns needed for grammatical correctness of the query, but these
62	        # columns are not included in self.select.
63	        self.select = None
64	        self.annotation_col_map = None
65	        self.klass_info = None
66	        self._meta_ordering = None
67	
68	    def __repr__(self):
69	        return (
70	            f"<{self.__class__.__qualname__} "
71	            f"model={self.query.model.__qualname__} "
72	            f"connection={self.connection!r} using={self.using!r}>"
73	        )
74	
75	    def setup_query(self, with_col_aliases=False):
76	        if all(self.query.alias_refcount[a] == 0 for a in self.query.alias_map):
77	            self.query.get_initial_alias()
78	        self.select, self.klass_info, self.annotation_col_map = self.get_select(
79	            with_col_aliases=with_col_aliases,
80	        )
81	        self.col_count = len(self.select)
82	
83	    def pre_sql_setup(self, with_col_aliases=False):
84	        """
85	        Do any necessary class setup immediately prior to producing SQL. This
86	        is for things that can't necessarily be done in __init__ because we
87	        might not have all the pieces in place at that time.
88	        """
89	        self.setup_query(with_col_aliases=with_col_aliases)
90	        order_by = self.get_order_by()
91	        self.where, self.having, self.qualify = self.query.where.split_having_qualify(
92	            must_group_by=self.query.group_by is not None
93	        )
94	        extra_select = self.get_extra_select(order_by, self.select)
95	        self.has_extra_select = bool(extra_select)
96	        group_by = self.get_group_by(self.select + extra_select, order_by)
97	        return extra_select, order_by, group_by
98	
99	    def get_group_by(self, select, order_by):
100	        """
101	        Return a list of 2-tuples of form (sql, params).
102	
103	        The logic of what exactly the GROUP BY clause contains is hard
104	        to describe in other words than "if it passes the test suite,
105	        then it is correct".
106	        """
107	        # Some examples:
108	        #     SomeModel.objects.annotate(Count('somecol'))
109	        #     GROUP BY: all fields of the model
110	        #
111	        #    SomeModel.objects.values('name').annotate(Count('somecol'))
112	        #    GROUP BY: name
113	        #
114	        #    SomeModel.objects.annotate(Count('somecol')).values('name')
115	        #    GROUP BY: all cols of the model
116	        #
117	        #    SomeModel.objects.values('name', 'pk')
118	        #    .annotate(Count('somecol')).values('pk')
119	        #    GROUP BY: name, pk
120	        #
121	        #    SomeModel.objects.values('name').annotate(Count('somecol')).values('pk')
122	        #    GROUP BY: name, pk
123	        #
124	        # In fact, the self.query.group_by is the minimal set to GROUP BY. It
125	        # can't be ever restricted to a smaller set, but additional columns in
126	        # HAVING, ORDER BY, and SELECT clauses are added to it. Unfortunately
127	        # the end result is that it is impossible to force the query to have
128	        # a chosen GROUP BY clause - you can almost do this by using the form:
129	        #     .values(*wanted_cols).annotate(AnAggregate())
130	        # but any later annotations, extra selects, values calls that
131	        # refer some column outside of the wanted_cols, order_by, or even
132	        # filter calls can alter the GROUP BY clause.
133	
134	        # The query.group_by is either None (no GROUP BY at all), True
135	        # (group by select fields), or a list of expressions to be added
136	        # to the group by.
137	        if self.query.group_by is None:
138	            return []
139	        expressions = []
140	        group_by_refs = set()
141	        if self.query.group_by is not True:
142	            # If the group by is set to a list (by .values() call most likely),
143	            # then we need to add everything in it to the GROUP BY clause.
144	            # Backwards compatibility hack for setting query.group_by. Remove
145	            # when we have public API way of forcing the GROUP BY clause.
146	            # Converts string references to expressions.
147	            for expr in self.query.group_by:
148	                if not hasattr(expr, "as_sql"):
149	                    expr = self.query.resolve_ref(expr)

27	class Join:
28	    """
29	    Used by sql.Query and sql.SQLCompiler to generate JOIN clauses into the
30	    FROM entry. For example, the SQL generated could be
31	        LEFT OUTER JOIN "sometable" T1
32	        ON ("othertable"."sometable_id" = "sometable"."id")
33	
34	    This class is primarily used in Query.alias_map. All entries in alias_map
35	    must be Join compatible by providing the following attributes and methods:
36	        - table_name (string)
37	        - table_alias (possible alias for the table, can be None)
38	        - join_type (can be None for those entries that aren't joined from
39	          anything)
40	        - parent_alias (which table is this join's parent, can be None
41	          similarly to join_type)
42	        - as_sql()
43	        - relabeled_clone()
44	    """
45	
46	    def __init__(
47	        self,
48	        table_name,
49	        parent_alias,
50	        table_alias,
51	        join_type,
52	        join_field,
53	        nullable,
54	        filtered_relation=None,
55	    ):
56	        # Join table
57	        self.table_name = table_name
58	        self.parent_alias = parent_alias
59	        # Note: table_alias is not necessarily known at instantiation time.
60	        self.table_alias = table_alias
61	        # LOUTER or INNER
62	        self.join_type = join_type
63	        # A list of 2-tuples to use in the ON clause of the JOIN.
64	        # Each 2-tuple will create one join condition in the ON clause.
65	        self.join_fields = join_field.get_joining_fields()
66	        self.join_cols = tuple(
67	            (lhs_field.column, rhs_field.column)
68	            for lhs_field, rhs_field in self.join_fields
69	        )
70	        # Along which field (or ForeignObjectRel in the reverse join case)
71	        self.join_field = join_field
72	        # Is this join nullabled?
73	        self.nullable = nullable
74	        self.filtered_relation = filtered_relation
75	
76	    def as_sql(self, compiler, connection):
77	        """
78	        Generate the full
79	           LEFT OUTER JOIN sometable
80	           ON sometable.somecol = othertable.othercol, params
81	        clause for this join.
82	        """
83	        join_conditions = []
84	        params = []
85	        qn = compiler.quote_name
86	        # Add a join condition for each pair of joining columns.
87	        for lhs, rhs in self.join_fields:
88	            lhs, rhs = connection.ops.prepare_join_on_clause(
89	                self.parent_alias, lhs, self.table_alias, rhs
90	            )
91	            lhs_sql, lhs_params = compiler.compile(lhs)
92	            lhs_full_name = lhs_sql % lhs_params
93	            rhs_sql, rhs_params = compiler.compile(rhs)
94	            rhs_full_name = rhs_sql % rhs_params
95	            join_conditions.append(f"{lhs_full_name} = {rhs_full_name}")
96

98	        # get_extra_restriction() returns.

705	                setattr(self, parent_link.target_field.attname, value)

713	        def _is_unset(value):
714	            return (
715	                value is None
716	                # Empty value when db_default is used.
717	                or isinstance(value, DatabaseDefault)
718	            )

720	        return not (

722	            or (isinstance(pk_val, tuple) and any(_is_unset(f) for f in pk_val))
723	        )

725	    def get_deferred_fields(self):

728	        """

732	            if f.attname not in self.__dict__

741	        any database. The using parameter will override the default.
742	
743	        Fields can be used to specify which fields to reload. The fields
744	        should be an iterable of field attnames. If fields is None, then
745	        all non-deferred fields are reloaded.
746

748	        fetch mode), the deferred loading uses this method.

188	            )
189	
190	        if cls._meta.abstract:

201	                )

import collections
import json
import re
import warnings
from functools import partial
from itertools import chain

from django.core.exceptions import EmptyResultSet, FieldError, FullResultSet
from django.db import DatabaseError, NotSupportedError
from django.db.models.constants import LOOKUP_SEP
from django.db.models.expressions import ColPairs, F, OrderBy, RawSQL, Ref, Value
from django.db.models.fields import AutoField, composite
from django.db.models.functions import Cast, Random
from django.db.models.lookups import Lookup
from django.db.models.query_utils import select_related_descend
from django.db.models.sql.constants import (
    CURSOR,
    GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE,
    MULTI,
    NO_RESULTS,
    ORDER_DIR,
    ROW_COUNT,
    SINGLE,
)
from django.db.models.sql.query import Query, get_order_dir
from django.db.transaction import TransactionManagementError
from django.utils.deprecation import RemovedInDjango70Warning
from django.utils.functional import cached_property
from django.utils.hashable import make_hashable
from django.utils.regex_helper import _lazy_re_compile
from django.utils.warnings import django_file_prefixes


class PositionRef(Ref):
    def __init__(self, ordinal, refs, source):
        self.ordinal = ordinal
        super().__init__(refs, source)

    def as_sql(self, compiler, connection):
        return str(self.ordinal), ()


class SQLCompiler:
    # Multiline ordering SQL clause may appear from RawSQL.
    ordering_parts = _lazy_re_compile(
        r"^(.*)\s(?:ASC|DESC).*",
        re.MULTILINE | re.DOTALL,
    )

    def __init__(self, query, connection, using, elide_empty=True):
        self.query = query
        self.connection = connection
        self.using = using
        # Some queries, e.g. coalesced aggregation, need to be executed even if
        # they would return an empty result set.
        self.elide_empty = elide_empty
        self.quote_cache = {"*": "*"}
        # The select, klass_info, and annotations are needed by
        # QuerySet.iterator() these are set as a side-effect of executing the
        # query. Note that we calculate separately a list of extra select
        # columns needed for grammatical correctness of the query, but these
        # columns are not included in self.select.
        self.select = None
        self.annotation_col_map = None
        self.klass_info = None
        self._meta_ordering = None

    def __repr__(self):
        return (
            f"<{self.__class__.__qualname__} "
            f"model={self.query.model.__qualname__} "
            f"connection={self.connection!r} using={self.using!r}>"
        )

    def setup_query(self, with_col_aliases=False):
        if all(self.query.alias_refcount[a] == 0 for a in self.query.alias_map):
            self.query.get_initial_alias()
        self.select, self.klass_info, self.annotation_col_map = self.get_select(
            with_col_aliases=with_col_aliases,
        )
        self.col_count = len(self.select)

    def pre_sql_setup(self, with_col_aliases=False):
        """
        Do any necessary class setup immediately prior to producing SQL. This
        is for things that can't necessarily be done in __init__ because we
        might not have all the pieces in place at that time.
        """
        self.setup_query(with_col_aliases=with_col_aliases)
        order_by = self.get_order_by()
        self.where, self.having, self.qualify = self.query.where.split_having_qualify(
            must_group_by=self.query.group_by is not None
        )
        extra_select = self.get_extra_select(order_by, self.select)
        self.has_extra_select = bool(extra_select)
        group_by = self.get_group_by(self.select + extra_select, order_by)
        return extra_select, order_by, group_by

    def get_group_by(self, select, order_by):
        """
        Return a list of 2-tuples of form (sql, params).

        The logic of what exactly the GROUP BY clause contains is hard
        to describe in other words than "if it passes the test suite,
        then it is correct".
        """
        # Some examples:
        #     SomeModel.objects.annotate(Count('somecol'))
        #     GROUP BY: all fields of the model
        #
        #    SomeModel.objects.values('name').annotate(Count('somecol'))
        #    GROUP BY: name
        #
        #    SomeModel.objects.annotate(Count('somecol')).values('name')
        #    GROUP BY: all cols of the model
        #
        #    SomeModel.objects.values('name', 'pk')
        #    .annotate(Count('somecol')).values('pk')
        #    GROUP BY: name, pk
        #

class Query(BaseExpression):
    """A single SQL query."""

    alias_prefix = "T"
    empty_result_set_value = None
    subq_aliases = frozenset([alias_prefix])

    compiler = "SQLCompiler"

    base_table_class = BaseTable
    join_class = Join

    default_cols = True
    default_ordering = True
    standard_ordering = True

    filter_is_sticky = False
    subquery = False
    contains_subquery = False

    # SQL-related attributes.
    # Select and related select clauses are expressions to use in the SELECT
    # clause of the query. The select is used for cases where we want to set up
    # the select clause to contain other than default fields (values(),
    # subqueries...). Note that annotations go to annotations dictionary.
    select = ()
    # The group_by attribute can have one of the following forms:
    #  - None: no group by at all in the query
    #  - A tuple of expressions: group by (at least) those expressions.
    #    String refs are also allowed for now.
    #  - True: group by all select fields of the model
    # See compiler.get_group_by() for details.
    group_by = None
    order_by = ()
    low_mark = 0  # Used for offset/limit.
    high_mark = None  # Used for offset/limit.
    distinct = False
    distinct_fields = ()
    select_for_update = False
    select_for_update_nowait = False
    select_for_update_skip_locked = False
    select_for_update_of = ()
    select_for_no_key_update = False
    select_related = False
    # Arbitrary limit for select_related to prevents infinite recursion.
    max_depth = 5
    # Holds the selects defined by a call to values() or values_list()
    # excluding annotation_select and extra_select.
    values_select = ()
    selected = None

    # SQL annotation-related attributes.
    annotation_select_mask = None
    _annotation_select_cache = None

    # Set combination attributes.
    combinator = None
    combinator_all = False
    combined_queries = ()

    # These are for extensions. The contents are more or less appended verbatim
    # to the appropriate clause.
    extra_select_mask = None
    _extra_select_cache = None

    extra_tables = ()
    extra_order_by = ()

    # A tuple that is a set of model field names and either True, if these are
    # the fields to defer, or False if these are the only fields to load.
    deferred_loading = (frozenset(), True)

    explain_info = None

    def __init__(self, model, alias_cols=True):
        self.model = model
        self.alias_refcount = {}
        # alias_map is the most important data structure regarding joins.
        # It's used for recording which joins exist in the query and what
        # types they are. The key is the alias of the joined table (possibly
        # the table name) and the value is a Join-like object (see
        # sql.datastructures.Join for more information).
        self.alias_map = {}
        # Whether to provide alias to columns during reference resolving.
        self.alias_cols = alias_cols
        # Sometimes the query contains references to aliases in outer queries
        # (as a result of split_exclude). Correct alias quoting needs to know
        # these aliases too. Map external tables to whether they are aliased.
        self.external_aliases = {}
        self.table_map = {}  # Maps table names to list of aliases.
        self.used_aliases = set()

        self.where = WhereNode()
        # Maps alias -> Annotation Expression.
        self.annotations = {}
        # These are for extensions. The contents are more or less appended
        # verbatim to the appropriate clause.
        self.extra = {}  # Maps col_alias -> (col_sql, params).

        self._filtered_relations = {}

    @property
    def output_field(self):
        if len(self.select) == 1:
            select = self.select[0]
            return getattr(select, "target", None) or select.field
        elif len(self.annotation_select) == 1:
            return next(iter(self.annotation_select.values())).output_field

    @cached_property
    def base_table(self):
        for alias in self.alias_map:
            return alias

    def __str__(self):
        """
        Return the query as a string of SQL with the parameter values
        substituted in (use sql_with_params() to see the unsubstituted string).

        Parameter values won't necessarily be quoted correctly, since that is
        done by the database interface at execution time.
        """
        sql, params = self.sql_with_params()
        return sql % params

    def sql_with_params(self):
        """
        Return the query as an SQL string and the parameters that will be
        substituted into the query.
        """
        return self.get_compiler(DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS).as_sql()

    def __deepcopy__(self, memo):
        """Limit the amount of work when a Query is deepcopied."""
        result = self.clone()
        memo[id(self)] = result
        return result

    def get_compiler(self, using=None, connection=None, elide_empty=True):
        if using is None and connection is None:
            raise ValueError("Need either using or connection")
        if using:
            connection = connections[using]
        return connection.ops.compiler(self.compiler)(
            self, connection, using, elide_empty
        )

    def get_meta(self):
        """
        Return the Options instance (the model._meta) from which to start
        processing. Normally, this is self.model._meta, but it can be changed
        by subclasses.
        """
        if self.model:
            return self.model._meta

    def clone(self):
        """
        Return a copy of the current Query. A lightweight alternative to
        deepcopy().
        """
        obj = Empty()
        obj.__class__ = self.__class__
        # Copy references to everything.
        obj.__dict__ = self.__dict__.copy()
        # Clone attributes that can't use shallow copy.
        obj.alias_refcount = self.alias_refcount.copy()
        obj.alias_map = self.alias_map.copy()
        obj.external_aliases = self.external_aliases.copy()
        obj.table_map = self.table_map.copy()
        obj.where = self.where.clone()
        obj.annotations = self.annotations.copy()
        if self.annotation_select_mask is not None:
            obj.annotation_select_mask = self.annotation_select_mask.copy()
        if self.combined_queries:
            obj.combined_queries = tuple(
                [query.clone() for query in self.combined_queries]
            )
        # _annotation_select_cache cannot be copied, as doing so breaks the
        # (necessary) state in which both annotations and
        # _annotation_select_cache point to the same underlying objects.
        # It will get re-populated in the cloned queryset the next time it's
        # used.
        obj._annotation_select_cache = None
        obj.extra = self.extra.copy()
        if self.extra_select_mask is not None:
            obj.extra_select_mask = self.extra_select_mask.copy()
        if self._extra_select_cache is not None:
            obj._extra_select_cache = self._extra_select_cache.copy()
        if self.select_related is not False:
            # Use deepcopy because select_related stores fields in nested
            # dicts.
            obj.select_related = copy.deepcopy(obj.select_related)
        if "subq_aliases" in self.__dict__:
            obj.subq_aliases = self.subq_aliases.copy()
        obj.used_aliases = self.used_aliases.copy()
        obj._filtered_relations = self._filtered_relations.copy()
        # Clear the cached_property, if it exists.

"""
The main QuerySet implementation. This provides the public API for the ORM.
"""

import copy
import operator
import warnings
from contextlib import nullcontext
from functools import reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from weakref import ref as weak_ref

from asgiref.sync import sync_to_async

import django
from django.conf import settings
from django.core import exceptions
from django.db import (
    DJANGO_VERSION_PICKLE_KEY,
    IntegrityError,
    NotSupportedError,
    connections,
    router,
    transaction,
)
from django.db.models import AutoField, DateField, DateTimeField, Field, Max, sql
from django.db.models.constants import LOOKUP_SEP, OnConflict
from django.db.models.deletion import Collector
from django.db.models.expressions import Case, DatabaseDefault, F, OrderBy, Value, When
from django.db.models.fetch_modes import FETCH_ONE
from django.db.models.functions import Cast, Trunc
from django.db.models.query_utils import PROHIBITED_FILTER_KWARGS, FilteredRelation, Q
from django.db.models.sql.constants import GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE, ROW_COUNT
from django.db.models.utils import (
    AltersData,
    create_namedtuple_class,
    resolve_callables,
)
from django.utils import timezone
from django.utils.deprecation import RemovedInDjango70Warning, RemovedInDjango71Warning
from django.utils.functional import cached_property
from django.utils.warnings import django_file_prefixes

# The maximum number of results to fetch in a get() query.
MAX_GET_RESULTS = 21

# The maximum number of items to display in a QuerySet.__repr__
REPR_OUTPUT_SIZE = 20

DEFAULT_FETCH_MODE = FETCH_ONE


class BaseIterable:
    def __init__(
        self, queryset, chunked_fetch=False, chunk_size=GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE
    ):
        self.queryset = queryset
        self.chunked_fetch = chunked_fetch
        self.chunk_size = chunk_size

    async def _async_generator(self):
        # Generators don't actually start running until the first time you call
        # next() on them, so make the generator object in the async thread and
        # then repeatedly dispatch to it in a sync thread.
        sync_generator = self.__iter__()

        def next_slice(gen):
            return list(islice(gen, self.chunk_size))

        while True:
            chunk = await sync_to_async(next_slice)(sync_generator)
            for item in chunk:
                yield item
            if len(chunk) < self.chunk_size:
                break

    # __aiter__() is a *synchronous* method that has to then return an
    # *asynchronous* iterator/generator. Thus, nest an async generator inside
    # it.
    # This is a generic iterable converter for now, and is going to suffer a
    # performance penalty on large sets of items due to the cost of crossing
    # over the sync barrier for each chunk. Custom __aiter__() methods should
    # be added to each Iterable subclass, but that needs some work in the
    # Compiler first.
    def __aiter__(self):
        return self._async_generator()


class ModelIterable(BaseIterable):
    """Iterable that yields a model instance for each row."""

    def __iter__(self):
        queryset = self.queryset
        db = queryset.db
        compiler = queryset.query.get_compiler(using=db)
        fetch_mode = queryset._fetch_mode
        # Execute the query. This will also fill compiler.select, klass_info,
        # and annotations.
        results = compiler.execute_sql(
            chunked_fetch=self.chunked_fetch, chunk_size=self.chunk_size
        )
        select, klass_info, annotation_col_map = (
            compiler.select,
            compiler.klass_info,
            compiler.annotation_col_map,
        )
        model_cls = klass_info["model"]
        select_fields = klass_info["select_fields"]
        model_fields_start, model_fields_end = select_fields[0], select_fields[-1] + 1
        init_list = [
            f[0].target.attname for f in select[model_fields_start:model_fields_end]
        ]
        related_populators = get_related_populators(klass_info, select, db, fetch_mode)
        known_related_objects = [
            (
                field,
                related_objs,
                attnames := [
                    (
                        field.attname

241	    def get_select(self, with_col_aliases=False):
242	        """
243	        Return three values:
244	        - a list of 3-tuples of (expression, (sql, params), alias)
245	        - a klass_info structure,
246	        - a dictionary of annotations
247	
248	        The (sql, params) is what the expression will produce, and alias is the
249	        "AS alias" for the column (possibly None).
250	
251	        The klass_info structure contains the following information:
252	        - The base model of the query.
253	        - Which columns for that model are present in the query (by
254	          position of the select clause).
255	        - related_klass_infos: [f, klass_info] to descent into
256	
257	        The annotations is a dictionary of {'attname': column position} values.
258	        """
259	        select = []
260	        klass_info = None
261	        annotations = {}
262	        assert not (self.query.select and self.query.default_cols)
263	        select_mask = self.query.get_select_mask()
264	        if self.query.default_cols:
265	            cols = self.get_default_columns(select_mask)
266	        else:
267	            # self.query.select is a special case. These columns never go to
268	            # any model.
269	            cols = self.query.select
270	        selected = []
271	        select_fields = None
272	        if self.query.selected is None:
273	            selected = [
274	                *(
275	                    (alias, RawSQL(*args))
276	                    for alias, args in self.query.extra_select.items()
277	                ),
278	                *((None, col) for col in cols),
279	                *self.query.annotation_select.items(),
280	            ]
281	            select_fields = list(
282	                range(
283	                    len(self.query.extra_select),
284	                    len(self.query.extra_select) + len(cols),
285	                )
286	            )
287	        else:
288	            select_fields = []
289	            for index, (alias, expression) in enumerate(self.query.selected.items()):
290	                # Reference to an annotation.
291	                if isinstance(expression, str):
292	                    expression = self.query.annotations[expression]
293	                # Reference to a column.
294	                elif isinstance(expression, int):
295	                    select_fields.append(index)
296	                    expression = cols[expression]
297	                # ColPairs cannot be aliased.
298	                if isinstance(expression, ColPairs):
299	                    alias = None
300	                selected.append((alias, expression))
301	        if select_fields:
302	            klass_info = {"model": self.query.model, "select_fields": select_fields}
303	
304	        for select_idx, (alias, expression) in enumerate(selected):
305	            if alias:
306	                annotations[alias] = select_idx
307	            select.append((expression, alias))
308	
309	        if self.query.select_related:
310	            related_klass_infos = self.get_related_selections(select, select_mask)
311	            klass_info["related_klass_infos"] = related_klass_infos
312	
313	            self.get_select_from_parent(klass_info)
314	
315	        ret = []
316	        col_idx = 1
317	        for col, alias in select:
318	            try:
319	                sql, params = self.compile(col)
320	            except EmptyResultSet:
321	                empty_result_set_value = getattr(
322	                    col, "empty_result_set_value", NotImplemented
323	                )
324	                if empty_result_set_value is NotImplemented:
325	                    # Select a predicate that's always False.
326	                    sql, params = "0", ()
327	                else:
328	                    sql, params = self.compile(Value(empty_result_set_value))
329	            except FullResultSet:
330	                sql, params = self.compile(Value(True))
331	            else:
332	                sql, params = col.select_format(self, sql, params)
333	            if alias is None and with_col_aliases:
334	                alias = f"col{col_idx}"
335	                col_idx += 1
336	            ret.append((col, (sql, params), alias))
337	        return ret, klass_info, annotations
338	
339	    def _order_by_pairs(self):
340	        if self.query.extra_order_by:
341	            ordering = self.query.extra_order_by
342	        elif not self.query.default_ordering:
343	            ordering = self.query.order_by
344	        elif self.query.order_by:
345	            ordering = self.query.order_by
346	        elif (meta := self.query.get_meta()) and meta.ordering:
347	            ordering = meta.ordering
348	            self._meta_ordering = ordering
349	        else:
350	            ordering = []
351	        if self.query.standard_ordering:
352	            default_order, _ = ORDER_DIR["ASC"]
353	        else:
354	            default_order, _ = ORDER_DIR["DESC"]
355	
356	        selected_exprs = {}
357	        # Avoid computing `selected_exprs` if there is no `ordering` as it's
358	        # relatively expensive.
359	        if ordering and (select := self.select):
360	            for ordinal, (expr, _, alias) in enumerate(select, start=1):
361	                pos_expr = PositionRef(ordinal, alias, expr)
362	                if alias:
363	                    selected_exprs[alias] = pos_expr
364	                selected_exprs[expr] = pos_expr
365	
366	        for field in ordering:
367	            if hasattr(field, "resolve_expression"):
368	                if isinstance(field, Value):
369	                    # output_field must be resolved for constants.
370	                    field = Cast(field, field.output_field)
371	                if not isinstance(field, OrderBy):
372	                    field = field.asc()
373	                if not self.query.standard_ordering:
374	                    field = field.copy()
375	                    field.reverse_ordering()
376	                select_ref = selected_exprs.get(field.expression)
377	                if select_ref or (
378	                    isinstance(field.expression, F)
379	                    and (select_ref := selected_exprs.get(field.expression.name))
380	                ):
381	                    # Emulation of NULLS (FIRST|LAST) cannot be combined with
382	                    # the usage of ordering by position.
383	                    if (
384	                        field.nulls_first is None and field.nulls_last is None
385	                    ) or self.connection.features.supports_order_by_nulls_modifier:
386	                        field = field.copy()
387	                        field.expression = select_ref

389	                    # combined queries so fallback to it if emulation of NULLS
390	                    # handling is required.
391	                    elif self.query.combinator:
392	                        field = field.copy()
393	                        field.expression = Ref(select_ref.refs, select_ref.source)
394	                yield field, select_ref is not None
395	                continue
396	            if field == "?":  # random
397	                yield OrderBy(Random()), False
398	                continue
399	
400	            col, order = get_order_dir(field, default_order)
401	            descending = order == "DESC"
402	
403	            if select_ref := selected_exprs.get(col):
404	                # Reference to expression in SELECT clause
405	                yield (
406	                    OrderBy(
407	                        select_ref,
408	                        descending=descending,
409	                    ),
410	                    True,
411	                )
412	                continue
413	
414	            if expr := self.query.annotations.get(col):
415	                ref = col
416	                transforms = []
417	            else:
418	                ref, *transforms = col.split(LOOKUP_SEP)
419	                expr = self.query.annotations.get(ref)
420	            if expr:
421	                if self.query.combinator and self.select:
422	                    if transforms:
423	                        raise NotImplementedError(
424	                            "Ordering combined queries by transforms is not "
425	                            "implemented."
426	                        )
427	                    # Don't use the resolved annotation because other
428	                    # combined queries might define it differently.
429	                    expr = F(ref)
430	                if transforms:
431	                    for name in transforms:
432	                        expr = self.query.try_transform(expr, name)
433	                if isinstance(expr, Value):
434	                    # output_field must be resolved for constants.
435	                    expr = Cast(expr, expr.output_field)
436	                yield OrderBy(expr, descending=descending), False
437	                continue
438	
439	            if "." in field and field in self.query.extra_order_by:
440	                # This came in through an extra(order_by=...) addition. Pass it

433	    def chain(self, klass=None):
434	        """
435	        Return a copy of the current Query that's ready for another operation.
436	        The klass argument changes the type of the Query, e.g. UpdateQuery.
437	        """
438	        obj = self.clone()
439	        if klass and obj.__class__ != klass:
440	            obj.__class__ = klass
441	        if not obj.filter_is_sticky:
442	            obj.used_aliases = set()
443	        obj.filter_is_sticky = False
444	        if hasattr(obj, "_setup_query"):
445	            obj._setup_query()
446	        return obj

449	        clone = self.clone()
450	        clone.change_aliases(change_map)
451	        return clone

454	        if not self.alias_cols:

461	        """
462	        if not aggregate_exprs:
463	            return {}

465	        # resolving purpose to facilitate their subsequent removal.
466	        refs_subquery = False
467	        refs_window = False

469	        annotation_select_mask = self.annotation_select_mask
470	        for alias, aggregate_expr in aggregate_exprs.items():
471	            self.check_alias(alias)
472	            aggregate = aggregate_expr.resolve_expression(
473	                self, allow_joins=True, reuse=None, summarize=True
474	            )

476	                raise TypeError("%s is not an aggregate expression" % alias)

478	            # members of `aggregates` to resolve against each others.
479	            self.append_annotation_mask([alias])
480	            aggregate_refs = aggregate.get_refs()

482	                getattr(self.annotations[ref], "contains_subquery", False)
483	                for ref in aggregate_refs
484	            )
485	            refs_window |= any(
486	                getattr(self.annotations[ref], "contains_over_clause", True)
487	                for ref in aggregate_refs
488	            )
489	            aggregate = aggregate.replace_expressions(replacements)
490	            self.annotations[alias] = aggregate
491	            replacements[Ref(alias, aggregate)] = aggregate

493	        # against each other.
494	        aggregates = {alias: self.annotations.pop(alias) for alias in aggregate_exprs}
495	        self.set_annotation_mask(annotation_select_mask)
496	        # Existing usage of aggregation can be determined by the presence of
497	        # selected aggregates but also by filters against aliased aggregates.
498	        _, having, qualify = self.where.split_having_qualify()

500	            any(

502	                for annotation in self.annotations.values()
503	            )

505	        )

510	        }

def chain(self, klass=None):
        """
        Return a copy of the current Query that's ready for another operation.
        The klass argument changes the type of the Query, e.g. UpdateQuery.
        """
        obj = self.clone()
        if klass and obj.__class__ != klass:
            obj.__class__ = klass
        if not obj.filter_is_sticky:
            obj.used_aliases = set()
        obj.filter_is_sticky = False
        if hasattr(obj, "_setup_query"):
            obj._setup_query()
        return obj

    def relabeled_clone(self, change_map):
        clone = self.clone()
        clone.change_aliases(change_map)
        return clone

    def _get_col(self, target, field, alias):
        if not self.alias_cols:
            alias = None
        return target.get_col(alias, field)

    def get_aggregation(self, using, aggregate_exprs):
        """
        Return the dictionary with the values of the existing aggregations.
        """
        if not aggregate_exprs:
            return {}
        # Store annotation mask prior to temporarily adding aggregations for
        # resolving purpose to facilitate their subsequent removal.
        refs_subquery = False
        refs_window = False
        replacements = {}
        annotation_select_mask = self.annotation_select_mask
        for alias, aggregate_expr in aggregate_exprs.items():
            self.check_alias(alias)
            aggregate = aggregate_expr.resolve_expression(
                self, allow_joins=True, reuse=None, summarize=True
            )
            if not aggregate.contains_aggregate:
                raise TypeError("%s is not an aggregate expression" % alias)
            # Temporarily add aggregate to annotations to allow remaining
            # members of `aggregates` to resolve against each others.
            self.append_annotation_mask([alias])
            aggregate_refs = aggregate.get_refs()
            refs_subquery |= any(
                getattr(self.annotations[ref], "contains_subquery", False)
                for ref in aggregate_refs
            )
            refs_window |= any(
                getattr(self.annotations[ref], "contains_over_clause", True)
                for ref in aggregate_refs
            )
            aggregate = aggregate.replace_expressions(replacements)
            self.annotations[alias] = aggregate
            replacements[Ref(alias, aggregate)] = aggregate
        # Stash resolved aggregates now that they have been allowed to resolve
        # against each other.
        aggregates = {alias: self.annotations.pop(alias) for alias in aggregate_exprs}
        self.set_annotation_mask(annotation_select_mask)
        # Existing usage of aggregation can be determined by the presence of
        # selected aggregates but also by filters against aliased aggregates.
        _, having, qualify = self.where.split_having_qualify()
        has_existing_aggregation = (
            any(
                getattr(annotation, "contains_aggregate", True)
                for annotation in self.annotations.values()
            )
            or having
        )
        set_returning_annotations = {
            alias
            for alias, annotation in self.annotation_select.items()
            if getattr(annotation, "set_returning", False)
        }
        # Decide if we need to use a subquery.
        #
        # Existing aggregations would cause incorrect results as
        # get_aggregation() must produce just one result and thus must not use
        # GROUP BY.
        #
        # If the query has limit or distinct, or uses set operations, then
        # those operations must be done in a subquery so that the query
        # aggregates on the limit and/or distinct results instead of applying
        # the distinct and limit after the aggregation.
        if (
            isinstance(self.group_by, tuple)
            or self.is_sliced
            or has_existing_aggregation
            or refs_subquery
            or refs_window
            or qualify
            or self.distinct
            or self.combinator
            or set_returning_annotations
        ):
            from django.db.models.sql.subqueries import AggregateQuery

89	class ModelIterable(BaseIterable):
90	    """Iterable that yields a model instance for each row."""
91	
92	    def __iter__(self):
93	        queryset = self.queryset
94	        db = queryset.db
95	        compiler = queryset.query.get_compiler(using=db)
96	        fetch_mode = queryset._fetch_mode
97	        # Execute the query. This will also fill compiler.select, klass_info,
98	        # and annotations.
99	        results = compiler.execute_sql(
100	            chunked_fetch=self.chunked_fetch, chunk_size=self.chunk_size
101	        )
102	        select, klass_info, annotation_col_map = (
103	            compiler.select,
104	            compiler.klass_info,
105	            compiler.annotation_col_map,
106	        )
107	        model_cls = klass_info["model"]
108	        select_fields = klass_info["select_fields"]
109	        model_fields_start, model_fields_end = select_fields[0], select_fields[-1] + 1
110	        init_list = [
111	            f[0].target.attname for f in select[model_fields_start:model_fields_end]
112	        ]
113	        related_populators = get_related_populators(klass_info, select, db, fetch_mode)
114	        known_related_objects = [
115	            (
116	                field,
117	                related_objs,
118	                attnames := [
119	                    (
120	                        field.attname
121	                        if from_field == "self"
122	                        else queryset.model._meta.get_field(from_field).attname
123	                    )
124	                    for from_field in field.from_fields
125	                ],
126	                operator.attrgetter(*attnames),
127	            )
128	            for field, related_objs in queryset._known_related_objects.items()
129	        ]
130	        peers = []
131	        for row in compiler.results_iter(results):
132	            obj = model_cls.from_db(
133	                db,
134	                init_list,
135	                row[model_fields_start:model_fields_end],
136	                fetch_mode=fetch_mode,
137	            )
138	            if fetch_mode.track_peers:
139	                peers.append(weak_ref(obj))
140	                obj._state.peers = peers
141	            for rel_populator in related_populators:
142	                rel_populator.populate(row, obj)
143	            if annotation_col_map:
144	                for attr_name, col_pos in annotation_col_map.items():
145	                    setattr(obj, attr_name, row[col_pos])
146	
147	            # Add the known related objects to the model.
148	            for field, rel_objs, rel_attnames, rel_getter in known_related_objects:
149	                # Avoid overwriting objects loaded by, e.g., select_related().
150	                if field.is_cached(obj):
151	                    continue
152	                # Avoid fetching potentially deferred attributes that would
153	                # result in unexpected queries.
154	                if any(attname not in obj.__dict__ for attname in rel_attnames):
155	                    continue
156	                rel_obj_id = rel_getter(obj)
157	                try:
158	                    rel_obj = rel_objs[rel_obj_id]
159	                except KeyError:
160	                    pass  # May happen in qs1 | qs2 scenarios.
161	                else:
162	                    setattr(obj, field.name, rel_obj)
163	
164	            yield obj

167	class RawModelIterable(BaseIterable):
168	    """
169	    Iterable that yields a model instance for each row from a raw queryset.
170	    """
171	
172	    def __iter__(self):
173	        # Cache some things for performance reasons outside the loop.
174	        db = self.queryset.db
175	        query = self.queryset.query
176	        connection = connections[db]
177	        compiler = connection.ops.compiler("SQLCompiler")(query, connection, db)
178	        query_iterator = iter(query)
179	
180	        try:
181	            (
182	                model_init_names,
183	                model_init_pos,
184	                annotation_fields,
185	            ) = self.queryset.resolve_model_init_order()
186	            model_cls = self.queryset.model
187	            if any(
188	                f.attname not in model_init_names for f in model_cls._meta.pk_fields
189	            ):
190	                raise exceptions.FieldDoesNotExist(
191	                    "Raw query must include the primary key"
192	                )
193	            fields = [self.queryset.model_fields.get(c) for c in self.queryset.columns]
194	            cols = [f.get_col(f.model._meta.db_table) if f else None for f in fields]
195	            converters = compiler.get_converters(cols)
196	            if converters:
197	                query_iterator = compiler.apply_converters(query_iterator, converters)
198	            if compiler.has_composite_fields(cols):
199	                query_iterator = compiler.composite_fields_to_tuples(
200	                    query_iterator, cols
201	                )
202	            fetch_mode = self.queryset._fetch_mode
203	            peers = []
204	            for values in query_iterator:
205	                # Associate fields to values
206	                model_init_values = [values[pos] for pos in model_init_pos]
207	                instance = model_cls.from_db(
208	                    db, model_init_names, model_init_values, fetch_mode=fetch_mode
209	                )
210	                if fetch_mode.track_peers:
211	                    peers.append(weak_ref(instance))
212	                    instance._state.peers = peers
213	                if annotation_fields:
214	                    for column, pos in annotation_fields:
215	                        setattr(instance, column, values[pos])
216	                yield instance
217	        finally:
218	            # Done iterating the Query. If it has its own cursor, close it.
219	            if hasattr(query, "cursor") and query.cursor:
220	                query.cursor.close()

223	class ValuesIterable(BaseIterable):
224	    """
225	    Iterable returned by QuerySet.values() that yields a dict for each row.
226	    """
227	
228	    def __iter__(self):
229	        queryset = self.queryset
230	        query = queryset.query
231	        compiler = query.get_compiler(queryset.db)
232	
233	        if query.selected:
234	            names = list(query.selected)
235	        else:
236	            # extra(select=...) cols are always at the start of the row.
237	            names = [
238	                *query.extra_select,
239	                *query.values_select,
240	                *query.annotation_select,
241	            ]
242	        indexes = range(len(names))
243	        for row in compiler.results_iter(
244	            chunked_fetch=self.chunked_fetch, chunk_size=self.chunk_size
245	        ):
246	            yield {names[i]: row[i] for i in indexes}
247

249	class ValuesListIterable(BaseIterable):
250	    """
251	    Iterable returned by QuerySet.values_list(flat=False) that yields a tuple
252	    for each row.
253	    """
254	
255	    def __iter__(self):
256	        queryset = self.queryset
257	        query = queryset.query
258	        compiler = query.get_compiler(queryset.db)
259	        return compiler.results_iter(
260	            tuple_expected=True,
261	            chunked_fetch=self.chunked_fetch,
262	            chunk_size=self.chunk_size,
263	        )

266	class NamedValuesListIterable(ValuesListIterable):
267	    """
268	    Iterable returned by QuerySet.values_list(named=True) that yields a
269	    namedtuple for each row.
270	    """
271	
272	    def __iter__(self):
273	        queryset = self.queryset
274	        if queryset._fields:
275	            names = queryset._fields
276	        else:
277	            query = queryset.query
278	            names = [
279	                *query.extra_select,
280	                *query.values_select,
281	                *query.annotation_select,
282	            ]
283	        tuple_class = create_namedtuple_class(*names)
284	        new = tuple.__new__
285	        for row in super().__iter__():
286	            yield new(tuple_class, row)

757	    def as_sql(self, with_limits=True, with_col_aliases=False):
758	        """
759	        Create the SQL for this query. Return the SQL string and list of
760	        parameters.
761	
762	        If 'with_limits' is False, any limit/offset information is not included
763	        in the query.
764	        """
765	        refcounts_before = self.query.alias_refcount.copy()
766	        try:
767	            combinator = self.query.combinator
768	            extra_select, order_by, group_by = self.pre_sql_setup(
769	                with_col_aliases=with_col_aliases or bool(combinator),
770	            )
771	            for_update_part = None
772	            # Is a LIMIT/OFFSET clause needed?
773	            with_limit_offset = with_limits and self.query.is_sliced
774	            combinator = self.query.combinator
775	            features = self.connection.features
776	            if combinator:
777	                if not getattr(features, "supports_select_{}".format(combinator)):
778	                    raise NotSupportedError(
779	                        "{} is not supported on this database backend.".format(
780	                            combinator
781	                        )
782	                    )
783	                result, params = self.get_combinator_sql(
784	                    combinator, self.query.combinator_all
785	                )
786	            elif self.qualify:
787	                result, params = self.get_qualify_sql()
788	                order_by = None
789	            else:
790	                distinct_fields, distinct_params = self.get_distinct()
791	                # This must come after 'select', 'ordering', and 'distinct'
792	                # (see docstring of get_from_clause() for details).
793	                from_, f_params = self.get_from_clause()
794	                try:
795	                    where, w_params = (
796	                        self.compile(self.where) if self.where is not None else ("", [])
797	                    )
798	                except EmptyResultSet:
799	                    if self.elide_empty:
800	                        raise
801	                    # Use a predicate that's always False.
802	                    where, w_params = "0 = 1", []
803	                except FullResultSet:
804	                    where, w_params = "", []
805	                try:
806	                    having, h_params = (
807	                        self.compile(self.having)
808	                        if self.having is not None
809	                        else ("", [])
810	                    )
811	                except FullResultSet:
812	                    having, h_params = "", []
813	                result = ["SELECT"]
814	                params = []
815	
816	                if self.query.distinct:
817	                    distinct_result, distinct_params = self.connection.ops.distinct_sql(
818	                        distinct_fields,
819	                        distinct_params,
820	                    )
821	                    result += distinct_result
822	                    params += distinct_params
823	
824	                out_cols = []
825	                for _, (s_sql, s_params), alias in self.select + extra_select:
826	                    if alias:
827	                        s_sql = "%s AS %s" % (
828	                            s_sql,
829	                            self.connection.ops.quote_name(alias),
830	                        )
831	                    params.extend(s_params)
832	                    out_cols.append(s_sql)
833	
834	                result += [", ".join(out_cols)]
835	                if from_:
836	                    result += ["FROM", *from_]
837	                elif self.connection.features.bare_select_suffix:
838	                    result += [self.connection.features.bare_select_suffix]
839	                params.extend(f_params)
840	
841	                if self.query.select_for_update and features.has_select_for_update:
842	                    if (
843	                        self.connection.get_autocommit()
844	                        # Don't raise an exception when database doesn't
845	                        # support transactions, as it's a noop.
846	                        and features.supports_transactions
847	                    ):
848	                        raise TransactionManagementError(
849	                            "select_for_update cannot be used outside of a transaction."
850	                        )
851	
852	                    if (
853	                        with_limit_offset
854	                        and not features.supports_select_for_update_with_limit
855	                    ):
856	                        raise NotSupportedError(
857	                            "LIMIT/OFFSET is not supported with "
858	                            "select_for_update on this database backend."
859	                        )

    def add_filter(self, filter_lhs, filter_rhs):
        self.add_q(Q((filter_lhs, filter_rhs)))

    def add_q(self, q_object, reuse_all=False):
        """
        A preprocessor for the internal _add_q(). Responsible for doing final
        join promotion.
        """
        # For join promotion this case is doing an AND for the added q_object
        # and existing conditions. So, any existing inner join forces the join
        # type to remain inner. Existing outer joins can however be demoted.
        # (Consider case where rel_a is LOUTER and rel_a__col=1 is added - if
        # rel_a doesn't produce any rows, then the whole condition must fail.
        # So, demotion is OK.
        existing_inner = {
            a for a in self.alias_map if self.alias_map[a].join_type == INNER
        }
        if reuse_all:
            can_reuse = set(self.alias_map)
        else:
            can_reuse = self.used_aliases
        clause, _ = self._add_q(q_object, can_reuse)
        if clause:
            self.where.add(clause, AND)
        self.demote_joins(existing_inner)

    def build_where(self, filter_expr):
        return self.build_filter(filter_expr, allow_joins=False)[0]

    def clear_where(self):
        self.where = WhereNode()

    def _add_q(
        self,
        q_object,
        used_aliases,
        branch_negated=False,
        current_negated=False,
        allow_joins=True,
        split_subq=True,
        check_filterable=True,
        summarize=False,
        update_join_types=True,
    ):
        """Add a Q-object to the current filter."""
        connector = q_object.connector
        current_negated ^= q_object.negated
        branch_negated = branch_negated or q_object.negated
        target_clause = WhereNode(connector=connector, negated=q_object.negated)
        joinpromoter = JoinPromoter(
            q_object.connector, len(q_object.children), current_negated
        )
        for child in q_object.children:
            child_clause, needed_inner = self.build_filter(
                child,
                can_reuse=used_aliases,
                branch_negated=branch_negated,
                current_negated=current_negated,
                allow_joins=allow_joins,
                split_subq=split_subq,
                check_filterable=check_filterable,
                summarize=summarize,
                update_join_types=update_join_types,
            )
            joinpromoter.add_votes(needed_inner)
            if child_clause:
                target_clause.add(child_clause, connector)
        if update_join_types:
            needed_inner = joinpromoter.update_join_types(self)
        else:
            needed_inner = []
        return target_clause, needed_inner

    def add_filtered_relation(self, filtered_relation, alias):
        if "." in alias:
            raise ValueError(
                "FilteredRelation doesn't support aliases with periods "
                "(got %r)." % alias
            )
        self.check_alias(alias)
        filtered_relation.alias = alias
        relation_lookup_parts, relation_field_parts, _ = self.solve_lookup_type(
            filtered_relation.relation_name
        )
        if relation_lookup_parts:
            raise ValueError(
                "FilteredRelation's relation_name cannot contain lookups "
                "(got %r)." % filtered_relation.relation_name
            )
        for lookup in get_children_from_q(filtered_relation.condition):
            lookup_parts, lookup_field_parts, _ = self.solve_lookup_type(lookup)
            shift = 2 if not lookup_parts else 1
            lookup_field_path = lookup_field_parts[:-shift]
            for idx, lookup_field_part in enumerate(lookup_field_path):
                if len(relation_field_parts) > idx:
                    if relation_field_parts[idx] != lookup_field_part:
                        raise ValueError(
                            "FilteredRelation's condition doesn't support "
                            "relations outside the %r (got %r)."
                            % (filtered_relation.relation_name, lookup)
                        )
            if len(lookup_field_parts) > len(relation_field_parts) + 1:
                raise ValueError(
                    "FilteredRelation's condition doesn't support nested "
                    "relations deeper than the relation_name (got %r for "
                    "%r)." % (lookup, filtered_relation.relation_name)
                )
        filtered_relation = filtered_relation.clone()
        filtered_relation.condition = rename_prefix_from_q(
            filtered_relation.relation_name,
            alias,
            filtered_relation.condition,
        )
        self._filtered_relations[filtered_relation.alias] = filtered_relation

    def names_to_path(self, names, opts, allow_many=True, fail_on_missing=False):
        """
        Walk the list of names and turns them into PathInfo tuples. A single
        name in 'names' can generate multiple PathInfos (m2m, for example).

        'names' is the path of names to travel, 'opts' is the model Options we
        start the name resolving from, 'allow_many' is as for setup_joins().
        If fail_on_missing is set to True, then a name that can't be resolved
        will generate a FieldError.

        Return a list of PathInfo tuples. In addition return the final field
        (the last used join field) and target (which is a field guaranteed to
        contain the same value as the final field). Finally, return those names
        that weren't found (which are likely transforms and the final lookup).
        """
        path, names_with_path = [], []
        for pos, name in enumerate(names):
            cur_names_with_path = (name, [])
            if name == "pk" and opts is not None:
                name = opts.pk.name

            field = None
            filtered_relation = None
            try:
                if opts is None:
                    raise FieldDoesNotExist
                field = opts.get_field(name)
            except FieldDoesNotExist:
                if name in self.annotation_select:
                    field = self.annotation_select[name].output_field
                elif name in self._filtered_relations and pos == 0:
                    filtered_relation = self._filtered_relations[name]
                    if LOOKUP_SEP in filtered_relation.relation_name:
                        parts = filtered_relation.relation_name.split(LOOKUP_SEP)
                        filtered_relation_path, field, _, _ = self.names_to_path(
                            parts,
                            opts,
                            allow_many,
                            fail_on_missing,
                        )
                        path.extend(filtered_relation_path[:-1])
                    else:
                        field = opts.get_field(filtered_relation.relation_name)
            if field is not None:
                # Fields that contain one-to-many relations with a generic
                # model (like a GenericForeignKey) cannot generate reverse
                # relations and therefore cannot be used for reverse querying.
                if field.is_relation and not field.related_model:
                    raise FieldError(
                        "Field %r does not generate an automatic reverse "
                        "relation and therefore cannot be used for reverse "
                        "querying. If it is a GenericForeignKey, consider "
                        "adding a GenericRelation." % name
                    )
                try:
                    model = field.model._meta.concrete_model
                except AttributeError:
                    # QuerySet.annotate() may introduce fields that aren't
                    # attached to a model.
                    model = None
            else:
                # We didn't find the current field, so move position back
                # one step.
                pos -= 1
                if pos == -1 or fail_on_missing:
                    available = sorted(
                        [
                            *get_field_names_from_opts(opts),
                            *self.annotations,
                            *self._filtered_relations,
                        ]
                    )
                    raise FieldError(
                        "Cannot resolve keyword '%s' into field. "
                        "Choices are: %s" % (name, ", ".join(available))

1547	    def has_composite_fields(self, expressions):
1548	        # Check for composite fields before calling the relatively costly
1549	        # composite_fields_to_tuples.
1550	        return any(isinstance(expression, ColPairs) for expression in expressions)

1552	    def composite_fields_to_tuples(self, rows, expressions):
1553	        col_pair_slices = [
1554	            slice(i, i + len(expression))
1555	            for i, expression in enumerate(expressions)
1556	            if isinstance(expression, ColPairs)
1557	        ]
1558	
1559	        for row in map(list, rows):
1560	            for pos in col_pair_slices:
1561	                row[pos] = (tuple(row[pos]),)
1562	
1563	            yield row

1565	    def results_iter(
1566	        self,
1567	        results=None,
1568	        tuple_expected=False,
1569	        chunked_fetch=False,
1570	        chunk_size=GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE,
1571	    ):
1572	        """Return an iterator over the results from executing this query."""
1573	        if results is None:
1574	            results = self.execute_sql(
1575	                MULTI, chunked_fetch=chunked_fetch, chunk_size=chunk_size
1576	            )
1577	        fields = [s[0] for s in self.select[0 : self.col_count]]
1578	        converters = self.get_converters(fields)
1579	        rows = chain.from_iterable(results)
1580	        if converters:
1581	            rows = self.apply_converters(rows, converters)
1582	        if self.has_composite_fields(fields):
1583	            rows = self.composite_fields_to_tuples(rows, fields)
1584	        if tuple_expected:
1585	            rows = map(tuple, rows)
1586	        return rows

1604	           chunked reads when supported.

1606	        - ROW_COUNT: Retrieves the number of rows in the result.

1608	           caller's responsibility to close the cursor.

1626	        except Exception as e:
1627	            # Might fail for server-side cursors (e.g. connection closed)
1628	            try:
1629	                cursor.close()

1631	                raise e from None

1633	
1634	        if result_type == ROW_COUNT:

1636	                return cursor.rowcount
1637	            finally:
1638	                cursor.close()
1639	        if result_type == CURSOR:
1640	            # Give the caller the cursor to process and close.
1641	            return cursor
1642	        if result_type == SINGLE:
1643	            try:
1644	                val = cursor.fetchone()
1645	                if val:
1646	                    return val[0 : self.col_count]
1647	                return val
1648	            finally:
1649	                # done with the cursor
1650	                cursor.close()
1651	        if result_type == NO_RESULTS:
1652	            cursor.close()
1653	            return
1654	
1655	        result = cursor_iter(
1656	            cursor,
1657	            self.connection.features.empty_fetchmany_value,
1658	            self.col_count if self.has_extra_select else None,
1659	            chunk_size,
1660	        )
1661	        if not chunked_fetch or not self.connection.features.can_use_chunked_reads:

1665	            # unless the database doesn't support it.
