984	    def get_default_columns(
985	        self, select_mask, start_alias=None, opts=None, from_parent=None
986	    ):
987	        """
988	        Compute the default columns for selecting every field in the base
989	        model. Will sometimes be called to pull in related models (e.g. via
990	        select_related), in which case "opts" and "start_alias" will be given
991	        to provide a starting point for the traversal.
992	
993	        Return a list of strings, quoted appropriately for use in SQL
994	        directly, as well as a set of aliases used in the select statement (if
995	        'as_pairs' is True, return a list of (alias, col_name) pairs instead
996	        of strings as the first component and None as the second component).
997	        """
998	        result = []
999	        if opts is None:
1000	            if (opts := self.query.get_meta()) is None:
1001	                return result
1002	        start_alias = start_alias or self.query.get_initial_alias()
1003	        # The 'seen_models' is used to optimize checking the needed parent
1004	        # alias for a given field. This also includes None -> start_alias to
1005	        # be used by local fields.
1006	        seen_models = {None: start_alias}
1007	        select_mask_fields = set(composite.unnest(select_mask))
1008	
1009	        for field in opts.concrete_fields:
1010	            model = field.model._meta.concrete_model
1011	            # A proxy model will have a different model and concrete_model. We
1012	            # will assign None if the field belongs to this model.
1013	            if model == opts.model:
1014	                model = None
1015	            if (
1016	                from_parent
1017	                and model is not None
1018	                and issubclass(
1019	                    from_parent._meta.concrete_model, model._meta.concrete_model
1020	                )
1021	            ):
1022	                # Avoid loading data for already loaded parents.
1023	                # We end up here in the case select_related() resolution
1024	                # proceeds from parent model to child model. In that case the
1025	                # parent model data is already present in the SELECT clause,
1026	                # and we want to avoid reloading the same data again.
1027	                continue
1028	            if select_mask and field not in select_mask_fields:
1029	                continue
1030	            alias = self.query.join_parent_model(opts, model, start_alias, seen_models)
1031	            column = field.get_col(alias)
1032	            result.append(column)
1033	        return result
1034	
1035	    def get_distinct(self):
1036	        """
1037	        Return a quoted list of fields to use in DISTINCT ON part of the query.
1038	
1039	        This method can alter the tables in the query, and thus it must be
1040	        called before get_from_clause().
1041	        """
1042	        result = []
1043	        params = []
1044	        opts = self.query.get_meta()
1045	
1046	        for name in self.query.distinct_fields:
1047	            parts = name.split(LOOKUP_SEP)
1048	            _, targets, alias, joins, path, _, transform_function = self._setup_joins(
1049	                parts, opts, None
1050	            )
1051	            targets, alias, _ = self.query.trim_joins(targets, joins, path)
1052	            for target in targets:
1053	                if name in self.query.annotation_select:
1054	                    result.append(self.connection.ops.quote_name(name))
1055	                else:
1056	                    r, p = self.compile(transform_function(target, alias))
1057	                    result.append(r)
1058	                    params.append(p)
1059	        return result, params
1060	
1061	    def find_ordering_name(
1062	        self, name, opts, alias=None, default_order="ASC", already_seen=None
1063	    ):
1064	        """
1065	        Return the table alias (the name might be ambiguous, the alias will
1066	        not be) and column name for ordering by the given 'name' parameter.
1067	        The 'name' is of the form 'field1__field2__...__fieldN'.
1068	        """
1069	        name, order = get_order_dir(name, default_order)
1070	        descending = order == "DESC"
1071	        pieces = name.split(LOOKUP_SEP)
1072	        (
1073	            field,
1074	            targets,
1075	            alias,
1076	            joins,
1077	            path,
1078	            opts,
1079	            transform_function,
1080	        ) = self._setup_joins(pieces, opts, alias)
1081	
1082	        # If we get to this point and the field is a relation to another model,
1083	        # append the default ordering for that model unless it is the pk
1084	        # shortcut or the attribute name of the field that is specified or
1085	        # there are transforms to process.
1086	        if (
1087	            field.is_relation
1088	            and opts.ordering
1089	            and getattr(field, "attname", None) != pieces[-1]
1090	            and name != "pk"
1091	            and not getattr(transform_function, "has_transforms", False)
1092	        ):
1093	            # Firstly, avoid infinite loops.
1094	            already_seen = already_seen or set()
1095	            join_tuple = tuple(
1096	                getattr(self.query.alias_map[j], "join_cols", None) for j in joins
1097	            )
1098	            if join_tuple in already_seen:
1099	                raise FieldError("Infinite loop caused by ordering.")
1100	            already_seen.add(join_tuple)
1101	
1102	            results = []
1103	            for item in opts.ordering:
1104	                if hasattr(item, "resolve_expression") and not isinstance(
1105	                    item, OrderBy
1106	                ):
1107	                    item = item.desc() if descending else item.asc()
1108	                if isinstance(item, OrderBy):
1109	                    results.append(
1110	                        (item.prefix_references(f"{name}{LOOKUP_SEP}"), False)
1111	                    )
1112	                    continue
1113	                results.extend(
1114	                    (expr.prefix_references(f"{name}{LOOKUP_SEP}"), is_ref)
1115	                    for expr, is_ref in self.find_ordering_name(
1116	                        item, opts, alias, order, already_seen
1117	                    )
1118	                )
1119	            return results
1120	        targets, alias, _ = self.query.trim_joins(targets, joins, path)
1121	        return [
1122	            (OrderBy(transform_function(t, alias), descending=descending), False)
1123	            for t in targets
1124	        ]
1125	
1126	    def _setup_joins(self, pieces, opts, alias):
1127	        """
1128	        Helper method for get_order_by() and get_distinct().
1129	
1130	        get_ordering() and get_distinct() must produce same target columns on
1131	        same input, as the prefixes of get_ordering() and get_distinct() must
1132	        match. Executing SQL where this is not true is an error.
1133	        """
1134	        alias = alias or self.query.get_initial_alias()
1135	        field, targets, opts, joins, path, transform_function = self.query.setup_joins(
1136	            pieces, opts, alias
1137	        )
1138	        alias = joins[-1]
1139	        return field, targets, alias, joins, path, opts, transform_function
1140	
1141	    def get_from_clause(self):
1142	        """
1143	        Return a list of strings that are joined together to go after the
1144	        "FROM" part of the query, as well as a list any extra parameters that
1145	        need to be included. Subclasses, can override this to create a
1146	        from-clause via a "select".
1147	
1148	        This should only be called after any SQL construction methods that
1149	        might change the tables that are needed. This means the select columns,
1150	        ordering, and distinct must be done first.
1151	        """
1152	        result = []
1153	        params = []
1154	        # Copy alias_map to a tuple in case Join.as_sql() subclasses (objects
1155	        # in alias_map) alter compiler.query.alias_map. That would otherwise
1156	        # raise "RuntimeError: dictionary changed size during iteration".
1157	        for alias, from_clause in tuple(self.query.alias_map.items()):
1158	            if not self.query.alias_refcount[alias]:
1159	                continue
1160	            clause_sql, clause_params = self.compile(from_clause)
1161	            result.append(clause_sql)
1162	            params.extend(clause_params)
1163	        for t in self.query.extra_tables:
1164	            alias, _ = self.query.table_alias(t)
1165	            # Only add the alias if it's not already present (the table_alias()
1166	            # call increments the refcount, so an alias refcount of one means
1167	            # this is the only reference).
1168	            if (
1169	                alias not in self.query.alias_map
1170	                or self.query.alias_refcount[alias] == 1
1171	            ):
1172	                result.append(", %s" % self.quote_name(alias))
1173	        return result, params
1174	
1175	    def get_related_selections(
1176	        self,
1177	        select,
1178	        select_mask,
1179	        opts=None,
1180	        root_alias=None,
1181	        cur_depth=1,
1182	        requested=None,
1183	        restricted=None,
1184	    ):
1185	        """
1186	        Fill in the information needed for a select_related query. The current
1187	        depth is measured as the number of connections away from the root model
1188	        (for example, cur_depth=1 means we are looking at models with direct
1189	        connections to the root model).
1190	        """
1191	
1192	        def _get_field_choices():
1193	            direct_choices = (f.name for f in opts.fields if f.is_relation)
1194	            reverse_choices = (
1195	                f.field.related_query_name()
1196	                for f in opts.related_objects
1197	                if f.field.unique
1198	            )
1199	            return chain(
1200	                direct_choices, reverse_choices, self.query._filtered_relations
1201	            )
1202	
1203	        related_klass_infos = []
1204	        if not restricted and cur_depth > self.query.max_depth:
1205	            # We've recursed far enough; bail out.
1206	            return related_klass_infos
1207	
1208	        if not opts:
1209	            opts = self.query.get_meta()
1210	            root_alias = self.query.get_initial_alias()
1211	
1212	        # Setup for the case when only particular related fields should be
1213	        # included in the related selection.
1214	        fields_found = set()
1215	        if requested is None:
1216	            restricted = isinstance(self.query.select_related, dict)
1217	            if restricted:
1218	                requested = self.query.select_related
1219	
1220	        def get_related_klass_infos(klass_info, related_klass_infos):
1221	            klass_info["related_klass_infos"] = related_klass_infos
1222	
1223	        for f in opts.fields:
1224	            fields_found.add(f.name)
1225	
1226	            if restricted:
1227	                next = requested.get(f.name, {})
1228	                if not f.is_relation:
1229	                    # If a non-related field is used like a relation,
1230	                    # or if a single non-relational field is given.
1231	                    if next or f.name in requested:
1232	                        raise FieldError(
1233	                            "Non-relational field given in select_related: '%s'. "
1234	                            "Choices are: %s"
1235	                            % (
1236	                                f.name,
1237	                                ", ".join(_get_field_choices()) or "(none)",
1238	                            )
1239	                        )
1240	            else:
1241	                next = False
1242	
1243	            if not select_related_descend(f, restricted, requested, select_mask):
1244	                continue
1245	            related_select_mask = select_mask.get(f) or {}
1246	            klass_info = {
1247	                "model": f.remote_field.model,
1248	                "field": f,
1249	                "reverse": False,
1250	                "local_setter": f.set_cached_value,
1251	                "remote_setter": (
1252	                    f.remote_field.set_cached_value if f.unique else lambda x, y: None
1253	                ),
1254	                "from_parent": False,
1255	            }
1256	            related_klass_infos.append(klass_info)
1257	            select_fields = []
1258	            _, _, _, joins, _, _ = self.query.setup_joins([f.name], opts, root_alias)
1259	            alias = joins[-1]
1260	            columns = self.get_default_columns(
1261	                related_select_mask, start_alias=alias, opts=f.remote_field.model._meta
1262	            )
1263	            for col in columns:
1264	                select_fields.append(len(select))
1265	                select.append((col, None))
1266	            klass_info["select_fields"] = select_fields
1267	            next_klass_infos = self.get_related_selections(
1268	                select,
1269	                related_select_mask,
1270	                f.remote_field.model._meta,
1271	                alias,
1272	                cur_depth + 1,
1273	                next,
1274	                restricted,
1275	            )
1276	            get_related_klass_infos(klass_info, next_klass_infos)
1277	
1278	        if restricted:
1279	            related_fields = [
1280	                (o, o.field, o.related_model)
1281	                for o in opts.related_objects
1282	                if o.field.unique and not o.many_to_many
1283	            ]
1284	            for related_object, related_field, model in related_fields:
1285	                if not select_related_descend(
1286	                    related_object,
1287	                    restricted,
1288	                    requested,
1289	                    select_mask,
1290	                ):
1291	                    continue
1292	
1293	                related_select_mask = select_mask.get(related_object) or {}
1294	                related_field_name = related_field.related_query_name()
1295	                fields_found.add(related_field_name)
1296	
1297	                join_info = self.query.setup_joins(
1298	                    [related_field_name], opts, root_alias
1299	                )
1300	                alias = join_info.joins[-1]
1301	                from_parent = issubclass(model, opts.model) and model is not opts.model
1302	                klass_info = {
1303	                    "model": model,
1304	                    "field": related_field,
1305	                    "reverse": True,
1306	                    "local_setter": related_object.set_cached_value,
1307	                    "remote_setter": related_field.set_cached_value,
1308	                    "from_parent": from_parent,
1309	                }
1310	                related_klass_infos.append(klass_info)
1311	                select_fields = []
1312	                columns = self.get_default_columns(
1313	                    related_select_mask,
1314	                    start_alias=alias,
1315	                    opts=model._meta,
1316	                    from_parent=opts.model,
1317	                )
1318	                for col in columns:
1319	                    select_fields.append(len(select))
1320	                    select.append((col, None))
1321	                klass_info["select_fields"] = select_fields
1322	                next = requested.get(related_field_name, {})
1323	                next_klass_infos = self.get_related_selections(
1324	                    select,
1325	                    related_select_mask,
1326	                    model._meta,
1327	                    alias,
1328	                    cur_depth + 1,
1329	                    next,
1330	                    restricted,
1331	                )
1332	                get_related_klass_infos(klass_info, next_klass_infos)
1333	
1334	            def local_setter(final_field, obj, from_obj):
1335	                # Set a reverse fk object when relation is non-empty.
1336	                if from_obj:
1337	                    final_field.remote_field.set_cached_value(from_obj, obj)
1338	
1339	            def local_setter_noop(obj, from_obj):
1340	                pass
1341	
1342	            def remote_setter(name, obj, from_obj):
1343	                setattr(from_obj, name, obj)
1344	
1345	            for name in list(requested):
1346	                # Filtered relations work only on the topmost level.
1347	                if cur_depth > 1:
1348	                    break
1349	                if name in self.query._filtered_relations:
1350	                    fields_found.add(name)
1351	                    final_field, _, join_opts, joins, _, _ = self.query.setup_joins(
1352	                        [name], opts, root_alias
1353	                    )
1354	                    model = join_opts.model
1355	                    alias = joins[-1]
1356	                    from_parent = (
1357	                        issubclass(model, opts.model) and model is not opts.model
1358	                    )
1359	                    klass_info = {
1360	                        "model": model,
1361	                        "field": final_field,
1362	                        "reverse": True,
1363	                        "local_setter": (
1364	                            partial(local_setter, final_field)
1365	                            if len(joins) <= 2
1366	                            else local_setter_noop
1367	                        ),
1368	                        "remote_setter": partial(remote_setter, name),
1369	                        "from_parent": from_parent,
1370	                    }
1371	                    related_klass_infos.append(klass_info)
1372	                    select_fields = []
1373	                    field_select_mask = select_mask.get((name, final_field)) or {}
1374	                    columns = self.get_default_columns(
1375	                        field_select_mask,
1376	                        start_alias=alias,
1377	                        opts=model._meta,
1378	                        from_parent=opts.model,
1379	                    )
1380	                    for col in columns:
1381	                        select_fields.append(len(select))
1382	                        select.append((col, None))
1383	                    klass_info["select_fields"] = select_fields
1384	                    next_requested = requested.get(name, {})
1385	                    next_klass_infos = self.get_related_selections(
1386	                        select,
1387	                        field_select_mask,
1388	                        opts=model._meta,
1389	                        root_alias=alias,
1390	                        cur_depth=cur_depth + 1,
1391	                        requested=next_requested,
1392	                        restricted=restricted,
1393	                    )
1394	                    get_related_klass_infos(klass_info, next_klass_infos)
1395	            fields_not_found = set(requested).difference(fields_found)
1396	            if fields_not_found:
1397	                invalid_fields = ("'%s'" % s for s in fields_not_found)
1398	                raise FieldError(
1399	                    "Invalid field name(s) given in select_related: %s. "
1400	                    "Choices are: %s"
1401	                    % (
1402	                        ", ".join(invalid_fields),
1403	                        ", ".join(_get_field_choices()) or "(none)",
1404	                    )
1405	                )
1406	        return related_klass_infos

1408	    def get_select_for_update_of_arguments(self):
1409	        """
1410	        Return a quoted list of arguments for the SELECT FOR UPDATE OF part of
1411	        the query.
1412	        """
1413	
1414	        def _get_parent_klass_info(klass_info):
1415	            concrete_model = klass_info["model"]._meta.concrete_model
1416	            for parent_model, parent_link in concrete_model._meta.parents.items():
1417	                all_parents = parent_model._meta.all_parents
1418	                yield {
1419	                    "model": parent_model,
1420	                    "field": parent_link,
1421	                    "reverse": False,
1422	                    "select_fields": [
1423	                        select_index
1424	                        for select_index in klass_info["select_fields"]
1425	                        # Selected columns from a model or its parents.
1426	                        if (
1427	                            self.select[select_index][0].target.model == parent_model
1428	                            or self.select[select_index][0].target.model in all_parents
1429	                        )
1430	                    ],
1431	                }
1432	
1433	        def _get_first_selected_col_from_model(klass_info):
1434	            """
1435	            Find the first selected column from a model. If it doesn't exist,
1436	            don't lock a model.
1437	
1438	            select_fields is filled recursively, so it also contains fields
1439	            from the parent models.
1440	            """
1441	            concrete_model = klass_info["model"]._meta.concrete_model
1442	            for select_index in klass_info["select_fields"]:
1443	                if self.select[select_index][0].target.model == concrete_model:
1444	                    return self.select[select_index][0]
1445	
1446	        def _get_field_choices():
1447	            """Yield all allowed field paths in breadth-first search order."""
1448	            queue = collections.deque([(None, self.klass_info)])
1449	            while queue:
1450	                parent_path, klass_info = queue.popleft()
1451	                if parent_path is None:
1452	                    path = []
1453	                    yield "self"
1454	                else:
1455	                    field = klass_info["field"]
1456	                    if klass_info["reverse"]:
1457	                        field = field.remote_field
1458	                    path = [*parent_path, field.name]
1459	                    yield LOOKUP_SEP.join(path)
1460	                queue.extend(
1461	                    (path, klass_info)
1462	                    for klass_info in _get_parent_klass_info(klass_info)
1463	                )
1464	                queue.extend(
1465	                    (path, klass_info)
1466	                    for klass_info in klass_info.get("related_klass_infos", [])
1467	                )
1468	
1469	        if not self.klass_info:
1470	            return []
1471	        result = []
1472	        invalid_names = []
1473	        for name in self.query.select_for_update_of:
1474	            klass_info = self.klass_info
1475	            if name == "self":
1476	                col = _get_first_selected_col_from_model(klass_info)
1477	            else:
1478	                for part in name.split(LOOKUP_SEP):
1479	                    klass_infos = (
1480	                        *klass_info.get("related_klass_infos", []),
1481	                        *_get_parent_klass_info(klass_info),
1482	                    )
1483	                    for related_klass_info in klass_infos:

1514	    def get_converters(self, expressions):
1515	        i = 0
1516	        converters = {}
1517	
1518	        for expression in expressions:
1519	            if isinstance(expression, ColPairs):
1520	                cols = expression.get_source_expressions()
1521	                cols_converters = self.get_converters(cols)
1522	                for j, (convs, col) in cols_converters.items():
1523	                    converters[i + j] = (convs, col)
1524	                i += len(expression)
1525	            elif expression:
1526	                backend_converters = self.connection.ops.get_db_converters(expression)
1527	                field_converters = expression.get_db_converters(self.connection)
1528	                if backend_converters or field_converters:
1529	                    converters[i] = (backend_converters + field_converters, expression)
1530	                i += 1
1531	            else:
1532	                i += 1
1533	
1534	        return converters

1536	    def apply_converters(self, rows, converters):
1537	        connection = self.connection
1538	        converters = list(converters.items())
1539	        for row in map(list, rows):
1540	            for pos, (convs, expression) in converters:
1541	                value = row[pos]
1542	                for converter in convs:
1543	                    value = converter(value, expression, connection)
1544	                row[pos] = value
1545	            yield row

1547	    def has_composite_fields(self, expressions):
1548	        # Check for composite fields before calling the relatively costly
1549	        # composite_fields_to_tuples.
1550	        return any(isinstance(expression, ColPairs) for expression in expressions)
1551	
1552	    def composite_fields_to_tuples(self, rows, expressions):
1553	        col_pair_slices = [
1554	            slice(i, i + len(expression))
1555	            for i, expression in enumerate(expressions)
1556	            if isinstance(expression, ColPairs)
1557	        ]
1558	
1559	        for row in map(list, rows):
1560	            for pos in col_pair_slices:
1561	                row[pos] = (tuple(row[pos]),)
1562	
1563	            yield row

1565	    def results_iter(
1566	        self,
1567	        results=None,
1568	        tuple_expected=False,
1569	        chunked_fetch=False,
1570	        chunk_size=GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE,
1571	    ):
1572	        """Return an iterator over the results from executing this query."""
1573	        if results is None:
1574	            results = self.execute_sql(
1575	                MULTI, chunked_fetch=chunked_fetch, chunk_size=chunk_size
1576	            )
1577	        fields = [s[0] for s in self.select[0 : self.col_count]]
1578	        converters = self.get_converters(fields)
1579	        rows = chain.from_iterable(results)
1580	        if converters:
1581	            rows = self.apply_converters(rows, converters)
1582	        if self.has_composite_fields(fields):
1583	            rows = self.composite_fields_to_tuples(rows, fields)
1584	        if tuple_expected:
1585	            rows = map(tuple, rows)
1586	        return rows

1588	    def has_results(self):
1589	        """
1590	        Backends (e.g. NoSQL) can override this in order to use optimized
1591	        versions of "query has any results."
1592	        """
1593	        return bool(self.execute_sql(SINGLE))

1595	    def execute_sql(
1596	        self, result_type=MULTI, chunked_fetch=False, chunk_size=GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE
1597	    ):
1598	        """
1599	        Run the query against the database and return the result(s). The
1600	        return value depends on the value of result_type.
1601	
1602	        When result_type is:
1603	        - MULTI: Retrieves all rows using fetchmany(). Wraps in an iterator for
1604	           chunked reads when supported.
1605	        - SINGLE: Retrieves a single row using fetchone().
1606	        - ROW_COUNT: Retrieves the number of rows in the result.
1607	        - CURSOR: Runs the query, and returns the cursor object. It is the
1608	           caller's responsibility to close the cursor.
1609	        """
1610	        result_type = result_type or NO_RESULTS
1611	        try:
1612	            sql, params = self.as_sql()
1613	            if not sql:
1614	                raise EmptyResultSet
1615	        except EmptyResultSet:
1616	            if result_type == MULTI:
1617	                return iter([])
1618	            else:
1619	                return
1620	        if chunked_fetch:
1621	            cursor = self.connection.chunked_cursor()
1622	        else:
1623	            cursor = self.connection.cursor()
1624	        try:
1625	            cursor.execute(sql, params)
1626	        except Exception as e:
1627	            # Might fail for server-side cursors (e.g. connection closed)
1628	            try:
1629	                cursor.close()
1630	            except DatabaseError:
1631	                raise e from None
1632	            raise
1633	
1634	        if result_type == ROW_COUNT:
1635	            try:
1636	                return cursor.rowcount
1637	            finally:
1638	                cursor.close()
1639	        if result_type == CURSOR:
1640	            # Give the caller the cursor to process and close.
1641	            return cursor
1642	        if result_type == SINGLE:
1643	            try:
1644	                val = cursor.fetchone()
1645	                if val:
1646	                    return val[0 : self.col_count]
1647	                return val
1648	            finally:
1649	                # done with the cursor
1650	                cursor.close()
1651	        if result_type == NO_RESULTS:
1652	            cursor.close()
1653	            return
1654	
1655	        result = cursor_iter(
1656	            cursor,
1657	            self.connection.features.empty_fetchmany_value,
1658	            self.col_count if self.has_extra_select else None,
1659	            chunk_size,
1660	        )
1661	        if not chunked_fetch or not self.connection.features.can_use_chunked_reads:
1662	            # If we are using non-chunked reads, we return the same data
1663	            # structure as normally, but ensure it is all read into memory
1664	            # before going any further. Use chunked_fetch if requested,
1665	            # unless the database doesn't support it.
1666	            return list(result)
1667	        return result

1669	    def explain_query(self):
1670	        result = list(self.execute_sql())
1671	        # Some backends return 1 item tuples with strings, and others return
1672	        # tuples with integers and strings. Flatten them out into strings.
1673	        format_ = self.query.explain_info.format
1674	        output_formatter = json.dumps if format_ and format_.lower() == "json" else str
1675	        for row in result:
1676	            for value in row:
1677	                if not isinstance(value, str):
1678	                    yield " ".join([output_formatter(c) for c in value])
1679	                else:
1680	                    yield value

1684	    returning_fields = None

1687	    def field_as_sql(self, field, get_placeholder_sql, val):
1688	        """
1689	        Take a field and a value intended to be saved on that field, and
1690	        return placeholder SQL and accompanying params. Check for raw values,
1691	        fields with get_placeholder_sql(), and compilable defined in that
1692	        order.
1693	
1694	        When field is None, consider the value raw and use it as the
1695	        placeholder, with no corresponding parameters returned.
1696	        """
1697	        if field is None:
1698	            # A field value of None means the value is raw.
1699	            sql, params = val, []
1700	        elif get_placeholder_sql is not None:
1701	            # Some fields (e.g. geo fields) need special munging before
1702	            # they can be inserted.
1703	            sql, params = get_placeholder_sql(val, self, self.connection)
1704	        elif hasattr(val, "as_sql"):
1705	            # This is an expression, let's compile it.
1706	            sql, params = self.compile(val)
1707	        else:
1708	            # Return the common case for the placeholder
1709	            sql, params = "%s", [val]
1710	
1711	        # The following hook is only used by Oracle Spatial, which sometimes
1712	        # needs to yield 'NULL' and () as its placeholder and params instead
1713	        # of '%s' and (None,). The 'NULL' placeholder is produced earlier by
1714	        # OracleOperations.get_geom_placeholder(). The following line removes
1715	        # the corresponding None parameter. See ticket #10888.
1716	        params = self.connection.ops.modify_insert_params(sql, params)
1717	
1718	        return sql, params

1720	    def prepare_value(self, field, value):
1721	        """
1722	        Prepare a value to be used in a query by resolving it if it is an
1723	        expression and otherwise calling the field's get_db_prep_save().
1724	        """
1725	        if hasattr(value, "resolve_expression"):
1726	            value = value.resolve_expression(
1727	                self.query, allow_joins=False, for_save=True
1728	            )
1729	            # Don't allow values containing Col expressions. They refer to
1730	            # existing columns on a row, but in the case of insert the row
1731	            # doesn't exist yet.
1732	            if value.contains_column_references:
1733	                raise ValueError(
1734	                    'Failed to insert expression "%s" on %s. F() expressions '
1735	                    "can only be used to update, not to insert." % (value, field)
1736	                )
1737	            if value.contains_aggregate:
1738	                raise FieldError(
1739	                    "Aggregate functions are not allowed in this query "
1740	                    "(%s=%r)." % (field.name, value)
1741	                )
1742	            if value.contains_over_clause:
1743	                raise FieldError(
1744	                    "Window expressions are not allowed in this query (%s=%r)."
1745	                    % (field.name, value)
1746	                )
1747	        return field.get_db_prep_save(value, connection=self.connection)
1748	
1749	    def pre_save_val(self, field, obj):
1750	        """
1751	        Get the given field's value off the given obj. pre_save() is used for
1752	        things like auto_now on DateTimeField. Skip it if this is a raw query.
1753	        """
1754	        if self.query.raw:
1755	            return getattr(obj, field.attname)
1756	        return field.pre_save(obj, add=True)
1757	
1758	    def assemble_as_sql(self, fields, value_rows):
1759	        """
1760	        Take a sequence of N fields and a sequence of M rows of values, and
1761	        generate placeholder SQL and parameters for each field and value.
1762	        Return a pair containing:
1763	         * a sequence of M rows of N SQL placeholder strings, and
1764	         * a sequence of M rows of corresponding parameter values.
1765	
1766	        Each placeholder string may contain any number of '%s' interpolation
1767	        strings, and each parameter row will contain exactly as many params
1768	        as the total number of '%s's in the corresponding placeholder row.
1769	        """
1770	        if not value_rows:
1771	            return [], []
1772	
1773	        # list of (sql, [params]) tuples for each object to be saved
1774	        # Shape: [n_objs][n_fields][2]
1775	        get_placeholder_sqls = [
1776	            getattr(field, "get_placeholder_sql", None) for field in fields
1777	        ]
1778	        rows_of_fields_as_sql = (
1779	            (
1780	                self.field_as_sql(field, get_placeholder_sql, value)
1781	                for field, get_placeholder_sql, value in zip(
1782	                    fields, get_placeholder_sqls, row
1783	                )
1784	            )
1785	            for row in value_rows
1786	        )
1787	
1788	        # tuple like ([sqls], [[params]s]) for each object to be saved
1789	        # Shape: [n_objs][2][n_fields]
1790	        sql_and_param_pair_rows = (zip(*row) for row in rows_of_fields_as_sql)
1791	
1792	        # Extract separate lists for placeholders and params.
1793	        # Each of these has shape [n_objs][n_fields]
1794	        placeholder_rows, param_rows = zip(*sql_and_param_pair_rows)
1795	
1796	        # Params for each field are still lists, and need to be flattened.
1797	        param_rows = [[p for ps in row for p in ps] for row in param_rows]
1798	
1799	        return placeholder_rows, param_rows

1801	    def as_sql(self):
1802	        qn = self.quote_name
1803	        opts = self.query.get_meta()
1804	        insert_statement = self.connection.ops.insert_statement(
1805	            on_conflict=self.query.on_conflict,
1806	        )
1807	        result = ["%s %s" % (insert_statement, qn(opts.db_table))]
1808	
1809	        if fields := list(self.query.fields):
1810	            from django.db.models.expressions import DatabaseDefault
1811	
1812	            supports_default_keyword_in_bulk_insert = (
1813	                self.connection.features.supports_default_keyword_in_bulk_insert
1814	            )
1815	            value_cols = []
1816	            for field in list(fields):
1817	                field_prepare = partial(self.prepare_value, field)
1818	                field_pre_save = partial(self.pre_save_val, field)
1819	
1820	                field_values = []
1821	                for obj in self.query.objs:
1822	                    value = field_pre_save(obj)
1823	                    if not isinstance(value, DatabaseDefault):
1824	                        value = field_prepare(value)
1825	                    field_values.append(value)
1826	
1827	                if not field.has_db_default():
1828	                    value_cols.append(field_values)
1829	                    continue
1830	
1831	                # If all values are DEFAULT don't include the field and its
1832	                # values in the query as they are redundant and could prevent
1833	                # optimizations. This cannot be done if we're dealing with the
1834	                # last field as INSERT statements require at least one.
1835	                if len(fields) > 1 and all(
1836	                    isinstance(value, DatabaseDefault) for value in field_values
1837	                ):
1838	                    fields.remove(field)
1839	                    continue
1840	
1841	                if supports_default_keyword_in_bulk_insert:
1842	                    value_cols.append(field_values)
1843	                    continue
1844	
1845	                # If the field cannot be excluded from the INSERT for the
1846	                # reasons listed above and the backend doesn't support the
1847	                # DEFAULT keyword each values must be expanded into their
1848	                # underlying expressions.
1849	                prepared_db_default = field_prepare(field.db_default)
1850	                field_values = [
1851	                    (
1852	                        prepared_db_default
1853	                        if isinstance(value, DatabaseDefault)
1854	                        else value
1855	                    )
1856	                    for value in field_values
1857	                ]
1858	                value_cols.append(field_values)
1859	            value_rows = list(zip(*value_cols))
1860	            result.append("(%s)" % ", ".join(qn(f.column) for f in fields))
1861	        else:
1862	            # No fields were specified but an INSERT statement must include at
1863	            # least one column. This can only happen when the model's primary
1864	            # key is composed of a single auto-field so default to including it
1865	            # as a placeholder to generate a valid INSERT statement.
1866	            value_rows = [
1867	                [self.connection.ops.pk_default_value()] for _ in self.query.objs
1868	            ]
1869	            fields = [None]
1870	            result.append("(%s)" % qn(opts.pk.column))
1871	
1872	        # Currently the backends just accept values when generating bulk
1873	        # queries and generate their own placeholders. Doing that isn't
1874	        # necessary and it should be possible to use placeholders and
1875	        # expressions in bulk inserts too.
1876	        can_bulk = (
1877	            not self.returning_fields and self.connection.features.has_bulk_insert
1878	        )
1879	
1880	        placeholder_rows, param_rows = self.assemble_as_sql(fields, value_rows)
1881	
1882	        on_conflict_suffix_sql = self.connection.ops.on_conflict_suffix_sql(
1883	            fields,
1884	            self.query.on_conflict,
1885	            (f.column for f in self.query.update_fields),
1886	            (f.column for f in self.query.unique_fields),
1887	        )
1888	        if (
1889	            self.returning_fields
1890	            and self.connection.features.can_return_columns_from_insert
1891	        ):
1892	            if self.connection.features.can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert:
1893	                result.append(
1894	                    self.connection.ops.bulk_insert_sql(fields, placeholder_rows)
1895	                )
1896	                params = param_rows
1897	            else:
1898	                result.append("VALUES (%s)" % ", ".join(placeholder_rows[0]))
1899	                params = [param_rows[0]]
1900	            if on_conflict_suffix_sql:
1901	                result.append(on_conflict_suffix_sql)
1902	            # Skip empty r_sql to allow subclasses to customize behavior for
1903	            # 3rd party backends. Refs #19096.
1904	            r_sql, self.returning_params = self.connection.ops.returning_columns(
1905	                self.returning_fields
1906	            )
1907	            if r_sql:
1908	                result.append(r_sql)
1909	                params += [self.returning_params]
1910	            return [(" ".join(result), tuple(chain.from_iterable(params)))]
1911	
1912	        if can_bulk:
1913	            result.append(self.connection.ops.bulk_insert_sql(fields, placeholder_rows))
1914	            if on_conflict_suffix_sql:
1915	                result.append(on_conflict_suffix_sql)
1916	            return [(" ".join(result), tuple(p for ps in param_rows for p in ps))]
1917	        else:
1918	            if on_conflict_suffix_sql:
1919	                result.append(on_conflict_suffix_sql)
1920	            return [
1921	                (" ".join([*result, "VALUES (%s)" % ", ".join(p)]), vals)
1922	                for p, vals in zip(placeholder_rows, param_rows)

1925	    def execute_sql(self, returning_fields=None):
1926	        assert not (
1927	            returning_fields
1928	            and len(self.query.objs) != 1
1929	            and not self.connection.features.can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert
1930	        )
1931	        opts = self.query.get_meta()
1932	        self.returning_fields = returning_fields
1933	        cols = []
1934	        with self.connection.cursor() as cursor:
1935	            for sql, params in self.as_sql():
1936	                cursor.execute(sql, params)
1937	            if not self.returning_fields:
1938	                return []
1939	            obj_len = len(self.query.objs)
1940	            if (
1941	                self.connection.features.can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert
1942	                and obj_len > 1
1943	            ) or (
1944	                self.connection.features.can_return_columns_from_insert and obj_len == 1
1945	            ):
1946	                rows = self.connection.ops.fetch_returned_rows(
1947	                    cursor, self.returning_params
1948	                )
1949	                cols = [field.get_col(opts.db_table) for field in self.returning_fields]
1950	            elif returning_fields and isinstance(
1951	                returning_field := returning_fields[0], AutoField
1952	            ):
1953	                cols = [returning_field.get_col(opts.db_table)]
1954	                rows = [
1955	                    (
1956	                        self.connection.ops.last_insert_id(
1957	                            cursor,
1958	                            opts.db_table,
1959	                            returning_field.column,
1960	                        ),
1961	                    )
1962	                ]
1963	            else:
1964	                # Backend doesn't support returning fields and no auto-field
1965	                # that can be retrieved from `last_insert_id` was specified.
1966	                return []
1967	        converters = self.get_converters(cols)
1968	        if converters:
1969	            rows = self.apply_converters(rows, converters)
1970	        return list(rows)

1980	    @classmethod
1981	    def _expr_refs_base_model(cls, expr, base_model):
1982	        if isinstance(expr, Query):
1983	            return expr.model == base_model
1984	        if not hasattr(expr, "get_source_expressions"):
1985	            return False
1986	        return any(
1987	            cls._expr_refs_base_model(source_expr, base_model)
1988	            for source_expr in expr.get_source_expressions()

1995	            for expr in chain(
1996	                self.query.annotations.values(), self.query.where.children
1997	            )

2000	    def _as_sql(self, query):
2001	        delete = "DELETE FROM %s" % self.quote_name(query.base_table)
2002	        try:
2003	            where, params = self.compile(query.where)
2004	        except FullResultSet:
2005	            return delete, ()

2008	    def as_sql(self):

2011	        parameters.
2012	        """
2013	        if self.single_alias and (
2014	            self.connection.features.delete_can_self_reference_subquery
2015	            or not self.contains_self_reference_subquery
2016	        ):
2017	            return self._as_sql(self.query)
2018	        innerq = self.query.clone()
2019	        innerq.__class__ = Query
2020	        innerq.clear_select_clause()
2021	        pk = self.query.model._meta.pk
2022	        innerq.select = [pk.get_col(self.query.get_initial_alias())]
2023	        outerq = Query(self.query.model)
2024	        if not self.connection.features.update_can_self_select:
2025	            # Force the materialization of the inner query to allow reference
2026	            # to the target table on MySQL.
2027	            sql, params = innerq.get_compiler(connection=self.connection).as_sql()
2028	            innerq = RawSQL("SELECT * FROM (%s) subquery" % sql, params)
2029	        outerq.add_filter("pk__in", innerq)
2030	        return self._as_sql(outerq)

2233	class SQLAggregateCompiler(SQLCompiler):
2234	    def as_sql(self):
2235	        """
2236	        Create the SQL for this query. Return the SQL string and list of
2237	        parameters.
2238	        """
2239	        sql, params = [], []
2240	        for annotation in self.query.annotation_select.values():
2241	            ann_sql, ann_params = self.compile(annotation)
2242	            ann_sql, ann_params = annotation.select_format(self, ann_sql, ann_params)
2243	            sql.append(ann_sql)
2244	            params.extend(ann_params)
2245	        self.col_count = len(self.query.annotation_select)
2246	        sql = ", ".join(sql)
2247	        params = tuple(params)
2248	
2249	        inner_query_sql, inner_query_params = self.query.inner_query.get_compiler(
2250	            self.using,
2251	            elide_empty=self.elide_empty,
2252	        ).as_sql(with_col_aliases=True)
2253	        sql = "SELECT %s FROM (%s) subquery" % (sql, inner_query_sql)
2254	        params += inner_query_params
2255	        return sql, params

2258	def cursor_iter(cursor, sentinel, col_count, itersize):
2259	    """
2260	    Yield blocks of rows from a cursor and ensure the cursor is closed when
2261	    done.
2262	    """
2263	    try:
2264	        for rows in iter((lambda: cursor.fetchmany(itersize)), sentinel):
2265	            yield rows if col_count is None else [r[:col_count] for r in rows]
2266	    finally:
2267	        cursor.close()

817	    def _get_defer_select_mask(self, opts, mask, select_mask=None):
818	        if select_mask is None:
819	            select_mask = {}
820	        select_mask[opts.pk] = {}
821	        # All concrete fields and related objects that are not part of the
822	        # defer mask must be included. If a relational field is encountered it
823	        # gets added to the mask for it be considered if `select_related` and
824	        # the cycle continues by recursively calling this function.
825	        for field in opts.concrete_fields + opts.related_objects:
826	            field_mask = mask.pop(field.name, None)
827	            field_att_mask = None
828	            if field_attname := getattr(field, "attname", None):
829	                field_att_mask = mask.pop(field_attname, None)
830	            if field_mask is None and field_att_mask is None:
831	                select_mask.setdefault(field, {})
832	            elif field_mask:
833	                if not field.is_relation:
834	                    raise FieldError(next(iter(field_mask)))
835	                # Virtual fields such as many-to-many and generic foreign keys
836	                # cannot be effectively deferred. Historically, they were
837	                # allowed to be passed to QuerySet.defer(). Ignore such field
838	                # references until a layer of validation at mask alteration
839	                # time is eventually implemented.
840	                if field.many_to_many:
841	                    continue
842	                field_select_mask = select_mask.setdefault(field, {})
843	                related_model = field.related_model._meta.concrete_model
844	                self._get_defer_select_mask(
845	                    related_model._meta, field_mask, field_select_mask
846	                )
847	        # Remaining defer entries must be references to filtered relations
848	        # otherwise they are surfaced as missing field errors.
849	        for field_name, field_mask in mask.items():
850	            if filtered_relation := self._filtered_relations.get(field_name):
851	                relation = opts.get_field(filtered_relation.relation_name)
852	                field_select_mask = select_mask.setdefault((field_name, relation), {})
853	                related_model = relation.related_model._meta.concrete_model
854	                self._get_defer_select_mask(
855	                    related_model._meta, field_mask, field_select_mask
856	                )
857	            else:

882	        compute the columns to select from the database and also by the
883	        QuerySet class to work out which fields are being initialized on each

935	    def promote_joins(self, aliases):
936	        """
937	        Promote recursively the join type of given aliases and its children to
938	        an outer join. If 'unconditional' is False, only promote the join if
939	        it is nullable or the parent join is an outer join.
940	
941	        The children promotion is done to avoid join chains that contain a
942	        LOUTER b INNER c. So, if we have currently a INNER b INNER c and a->b
943	        is promoted, then we must also promote b->c automatically, or otherwise
944	        the promotion of a->b doesn't actually change anything in the query
945	        results.
946	        """
947	        aliases = list(aliases)
948	        while aliases:
949	            alias = aliases.pop(0)
950	            if self.alias_map[alias].join_type is None:
951	                # This is the base table (first FROM entry) - this table
952	                # isn't really joined at all in the query, so we should not
953	                # alter its join type.
954	                continue

956	            assert self.alias_map[alias].join_type is not None
957	            parent_alias = self.alias_map[alias].parent_alias
958	            parent_louter = (
959	                parent_alias and self.alias_map[parent_alias].join_type == LOUTER
960	            )
961	            already_louter = self.alias_map[alias].join_type == LOUTER
962	            if (self.alias_map[alias].nullable or parent_louter) and not already_louter:
963	                self.alias_map[alias] = self.alias_map[alias].promote()

965	                # refer to this one.

967	                    join
968	                    for join in self.alias_map

980	        demote a->b automatically, or otherwise the demotion of b->c doesn't
981	        actually change anything in the query results. .

1058	    def bump_prefix(self, other_query, exclude=None):
1059	        """
1060	        Change the alias prefix to the next letter in the alphabet in a way
1061	        that the other query's aliases and this query's aliases will not
1062	        conflict. Even tables that previously had no alias will get an alias
1063	        after this call. To prevent changing aliases use the exclude parameter.
1064	        """
1065	
1066	        def prefix_gen():
1067	            """
1068	            Generate a sequence of characters in alphabetical order:
1069	                -> 'A', 'B', 'C', ...
1070	
1071	            When the alphabet is finished, the sequence will continue with the
1072	            Cartesian product:
1073	                -> 'AA', 'AB', 'AC', ...
1074	            """
1075	            alphabet = ascii_uppercase
1076	            prefix = chr(ord(self.alias_prefix) + 1)
1077	            yield prefix
1078	            for n in count(1):
1079	                seq = alphabet[alphabet.index(prefix) :] if prefix else alphabet
1080	                for s in product(seq, repeat=n):
1081	                    yield "".join(s)
1082	                prefix = None
1083	
1084	        if self.alias_prefix != other_query.alias_prefix:
1085	            # No clashes between self and outer query should be possible.
1086	            return
1087	
1088	        # Explicitly avoid infinite loop. The constant divider is based on how
1089	        # much depth recursive subquery references add to the stack. This value
1090	        # might need to be adjusted when adding or removing function calls from
1091	        # the code path in charge of performing these operations.
1092	        local_recursion_limit = sys.getrecursionlimit() // 16
1093	        for pos, prefix in enumerate(prefix_gen()):
1094	            if prefix not in self.subq_aliases:
1095	                self.alias_prefix = prefix
1096	                break
1097	            if pos > local_recursion_limit:
1098	                raise RecursionError(
1099	                    "Maximum recursion depth exceeded: too many subqueries."
1100	                )
1101	        self.subq_aliases = self.subq_aliases.union([self.alias_prefix])
1102	        other_query.subq_aliases = other_query.subq_aliases.union(self.subq_aliases)
1103	        if exclude is None:
1104	            exclude = {}
1105	        self.change_aliases(
1106	            {
1107	                alias: "%s%d" % (self.alias_prefix, pos)
1108	                for pos, alias in enumerate(self.alias_map)
1109	                if alias not in exclude
1110	            }
1111	        )

1131	        added in compiler will not be seen by this method.
1132	        """
1133	        return len([1 for count in self.alias_refcount.values() if count])

1135	    def join(self, join, reuse=None):
1136	        """
1137	        Return an alias for the 'join', either reusing an existing alias for
1138	        that join or creating a new one. 'join' is either a base_table_class or
1139	        join_class.
1140	
1141	        The 'reuse' parameter can be either None which means all joins are
1142	        reusable, or it can be a set containing the aliases that can be reused.
1143	
1144	        A join is always created as LOUTER if the lhs alias is LOUTER to make
1145	        sure chains like t1 LOUTER t2 INNER t3 aren't generated. All new
1146	        joins are created as LOUTER if the join is nullable.
1147	        """
1148	        reuse_aliases = [
1149	            a
1150	            for a, j in self.alias_map.items()
1151	            if (reuse is None or a in reuse) and j == join
1152	        ]
1153	        if reuse_aliases:
1154	            if join.table_alias in reuse_aliases:
1155	                reuse_alias = join.table_alias
1156	            else:
1157	                # Reuse the most recent alias of the joined table
1158	                # (a many-to-many relation may be joined multiple times).
1159	                reuse_alias = reuse_aliases[-1]
1160	            self.ref_alias(reuse_alias)
1161	            return reuse_alias
1162	
1163	        # No reuse is possible, so we need a new alias.
1164	        alias, _ = self.table_alias(
1165	            join.table_name, create=True, filtered_relation=join.filtered_relation
1166	        )
1167	        if join.join_type:
1168	            if self.alias_map[join.parent_alias].join_type == LOUTER or join.nullable:
1169	                join_type = LOUTER
1170	            else:
1171	                join_type = INNER
1172	            join.join_type = join_type
1173	        join.table_alias = alias
1174	        self.alias_map[alias] = join
1175	        if filtered_relation := join.filtered_relation:
1176	            resolve_reuse = reuse
1177	            if resolve_reuse is not None:
1178	                resolve_reuse = set(reuse) | {alias}
1179	            joins_len = len(self.alias_map)
1180	            join.filtered_relation = filtered_relation.resolve_expression(
1181	                self, reuse=resolve_reuse
1182	            )
1183	            # Some joins were during expression resolving, they must be present
1184	            # before the one we just added.
1185	            if joins_len < len(self.alias_map):
1186	                self.alias_map[alias] = self.alias_map.pop(alias)
1187	        return alias

1189	    def join_parent_model(self, opts, model, alias, seen):
1190	        """
1191	        Make sure the given 'model' is joined in the query. If 'model' isn't
1192	        a parent of 'opts' or if it is None this method is a no-op.
1193	
1194	        The 'alias' is the root alias for starting the join, 'seen' is a dict
1195	        of model -> alias of existing joins. It must also contain a mapping
1196	        of None -> some alias. This will be returned in the no-op case.
1197	        """
1198	        if model in seen:
1199	            return seen[model]
1200	        chain = opts.get_base_chain(model)
1201	        if not chain:
1202	            return alias
1203	        curr_opts = opts
1204	        for int_model in chain:
1205	            if int_model in seen:
1206	                curr_opts = int_model._meta
1207	                alias = seen[int_model]
1208	                continue
1209	            # Proxy model have elements in base chain
1210	            # with no parents, assign the new options
1211	            # object and skip to the next base in that
1212	            # case
1213	            if not curr_opts.parents[int_model]:
1214	                curr_opts = int_model._meta
1215	                continue
1216	            link_field = curr_opts.get_ancestor_link(int_model)
1217	            join_info = self.setup_joins([link_field.name], curr_opts, alias)
1218	            curr_opts = int_model._meta
1219	            alias = seen[int_model] = join_info.joins[-1]
1220	        return alias or seen[None]

1226	                "Using percent signs in a column alias is deprecated.",
1227	                category=RemovedInDjango70Warning,
1228	                skip_file_prefixes=django_file_prefixes(),
1229	            )
1230	        if FORBIDDEN_ALIAS_PATTERN.search(alias):
1231	            raise ValueError(
1232	                "Column aliases cannot contain whitespace characters, hashes, "
1233	                # RemovedInDjango70Warning: When the deprecation ends, replace
1234	                # with:
1235	                # "control characters, quotation marks, semicolons, percent "

1240	    def add_annotation(self, annotation, alias, select=True):
1241	        """Add a single annotation expression to the Query."""
1242	        self.check_alias(alias)
1243	        annotation = annotation.resolve_expression(self, allow_joins=True, reuse=None)
1244	        if select:
1245	            self.append_annotation_mask([alias])
1246	        else:
1247	            self.set_annotation_mask(set(self.annotation_select).difference({alias}))
1248	        self.annotations[alias] = annotation
1249	        if select and self.selected:
1250	            self.selected[alias] = alias

1253	    def _subquery_fields_len(self):
1254	        if not self.has_select_fields or not self.select:
1255	            return len(self.model._meta.pk_fields)
1256	        return len(self.select) + sum(
1257	            len(expr.targets) - 1 for expr in self.select if isinstance(expr, ColPairs)

1260	    def resolve_expression(self, query, *args, **kwargs):
1261	        clone = self.clone()
1262	        # Subqueries need to use a different set of aliases than the outer
1263	        # query.
1264	        clone.bump_prefix(query)
1265	        clone.subquery = True
1266	        clone.where.resolve_expression(query, *args, **kwargs)
1267	        # Resolve combined queries.
1268	        if clone.combinator:
1269	            clone.combined_queries = tuple(
1270	                [
1271	                    combined_query.resolve_expression(query, *args, **kwargs)
1272	                    for combined_query in clone.combined_queries
1273	                ]
1274	            )
1275	        for key, value in clone.annotations.items():
1276	            resolved = value.resolve_expression(query, *args, **kwargs)
1277	            if hasattr(resolved, "external_aliases"):
1278	                resolved.external_aliases.update(clone.external_aliases)
1279	            clone.annotations[key] = resolved
1280	        # Outer query's aliases are considered external.
1281	        for alias, table in query.alias_map.items():
1282	            clone.external_aliases[alias] = (
1283	                isinstance(table, Join)
1284	                and table.join_field.related_model._meta.db_table != alias
1285	            ) or (
1286	                isinstance(table, BaseTable) and table.table_name != table.table_alias
1287	            )
1288	        return clone
1289	
1290	    def get_external_cols(self):
1291	        exprs = chain(self.annotations.values(), self.where.children)
1292	        return [
1293	            col
1294	            for col in self._gen_cols(exprs, include_external=True)
1295	            if col.alias in self.external_aliases
1296	        ]

1298	    def get_group_by_cols(self, wrapper=None):
1299	        # If wrapper is referenced by an alias for an explicit GROUP BY through
1300	        # values() a reference to this expression and not the self must be
1301	        # returned to ensure external column references are not grouped against
1302	        # as well.
1303	        external_cols = self.get_external_cols()
1304	        if any(col.possibly_multivalued for col in external_cols):
1305	            return [wrapper or self]
1306	        return external_cols

1308	    def as_sql(self, compiler, connection):
1309	        # Some backends (e.g. Oracle) raise an error when a subquery contains
1310	        # unnecessary ORDER BY clause.
1311	        if (
1312	            self.subquery
1313	            and not connection.features.ignores_unnecessary_order_by_in_subqueries
1314	        ):
1315	            self.clear_ordering(force=False)
1316	            for query in self.combined_queries:
1317	                query.clear_ordering(force=False)
1318	        sql, params = self.get_compiler(connection=connection).as_sql()
1319	        if self.subquery:
1320	            sql = "(%s)" % sql
1321	        return sql, params

1323	    def resolve_lookup_value(self, value, can_reuse, allow_joins, summarize=False):
1324	        if hasattr(value, "resolve_expression"):
1325	            value = value.resolve_expression(
1326	                self,
1327	                reuse=can_reuse,
1328	                allow_joins=allow_joins,
1329	                summarize=summarize,
1330	            )
1331	        elif isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
1332	            # The items of the iterable may be expressions and therefore need
1333	            # to be resolved independently.
1334	            values = (
1335	                self.resolve_lookup_value(sub_value, can_reuse, allow_joins, summarize)
1336	                for sub_value in value
1337	            )
1338	            type_ = type(value)
1339	            if hasattr(type_, "_make"):  # namedtuple
1340	                return type_(*values)
1341	            return type_(values)
1342	        return value

1344	    def solve_lookup_type(self, lookup, summarize=False):
1345	        """
1346	        Solve the lookup type from the lookup (e.g.: 'foobar__id__icontains').
1347	        """
1348	        lookup_splitted = lookup.split(LOOKUP_SEP)
1349	        if self.annotations:
1350	            annotation, expression_lookups = refs_expression(
1351	                lookup_splitted, self.annotations
1352	            )
1353	            if annotation:
1354	                expression = self.annotations[annotation]
1355	                if summarize:
1356	                    expression = Ref(annotation, expression)
1357	                return expression_lookups, (), expression
1358	        _, field, _, lookup_parts = self.names_to_path(lookup_splitted, self.get_meta())
1359	        field_parts = lookup_splitted[0 : len(lookup_splitted) - len(lookup_parts)]
1360	        if len(lookup_parts) > 1 and not field_parts:
1361	            raise FieldError(
1362	                'Invalid lookup "%s" for model %s".'
1363	                % (lookup, self.get_meta().model.__name__)
1364	            )
1365	        return lookup_parts, field_parts, False

1367	    def check_query_object_type(self, value, opts, field):
1368	        """
1369	        Check whether the object passed while querying is of the correct type.
1370	        If not, raise a ValueError specifying the wrong object.
1371	        """
1372	        if hasattr(value, "_meta"):
1373	            if not check_rel_lookup_compatibility(value._meta.model, opts, field):
1374	                raise ValueError(
1375	                    'Cannot query "%s": Must be "%s" instance.'
1376	                    % (value, opts.object_name)
1377	                )

1379	    def check_related_objects(self, field, value, opts):
1380	        """Check the type of object passed to query relations."""
1381	        if field.is_relation:
1382	            # Check that the field and the queryset use the same model in a
1383	            # query like .filter(author=Author.objects.all()). For example, the
1384	            # opts would be Author's (from the author field) and value.model
1385	            # would be Author.objects.all() queryset's .model (Author also).
1386	            # The field is the related field on the lhs side.
1387	            if (
1388	                isinstance(value, Query)
1389	                and not value.has_select_fields
1390	                and not check_rel_lookup_compatibility(value.model, opts, field)
1391	            ):
1392	                raise ValueError(
1393	                    'Cannot use QuerySet for "%s": Use a QuerySet for "%s".'
1394	                    % (value.model._meta.object_name, opts.object_name)
1395	                )
1396	            elif hasattr(value, "_meta"):
1397	                self.check_query_object_type(value, opts, field)
1398	            elif hasattr(value, "__iter__"):
1399	                for v in value:
1400	                    self.check_query_object_type(v, opts, field)

1402	    def check_filterable(self, expression):
1403	        """Raise an error if expression cannot be used in a WHERE clause."""
1404	        if hasattr(expression, "resolve_expression") and not getattr(
1405	            expression, "filterable", True
1406	        ):
1407	            raise NotSupportedError(
1408	                expression.__class__.__name__ + " is disallowed in the filter "
1409	                "clause."
1410	            )
1411	        if hasattr(expression, "get_source_expressions"):
1412	            for expr in expression.get_source_expressions():
1413	                self.check_filterable(expr)

1415	    def build_lookup(self, lookups, lhs, rhs):
1416	        """
1417	        Try to extract transforms and lookup from given lhs.
1418	
1419	        The lhs value is something that works like SQLExpression.
1420	        The rhs value is what the lookup is going to compare against.
1421	        The lookups is a list of names to extract using get_lookup()
1422	        and get_transform().
1423	        """
1424	        # __exact is the default lookup if one isn't given.
1425	        *transforms, lookup_name = lookups or ["exact"]
1426	        for name in transforms:
1427	            lhs = self.try_transform(lhs, name, lookups)
1428	        # First try get_lookup() so that the lookup takes precedence if the lhs
1429	        # supports both transform and lookup for the name.
1430	        lookup_class = lhs.get_lookup(lookup_name)
1431	        if not lookup_class:
1432	            # A lookup wasn't found. Try to interpret the name as a transform
1433	            # and do an Exact lookup against it.
1434	            lhs = self.try_transform(lhs, lookup_name)
1435	            lookup_name = "exact"
1436	            lookup_class = lhs.get_lookup(lookup_name)
1437	            if not lookup_class:
1438	                return
1439	
1440	        lookup = lookup_class(lhs, rhs)
1441	        # Interpret '__exact=None' as the sql 'is NULL'; otherwise, reject all
1442	        # uses of None as a query value unless the lookup supports it.
1443	        if lookup.rhs is None and not lookup.can_use_none_as_rhs:
1444	            if lookup_name not in ("exact", "iexact"):
1445	                raise ValueError("Cannot use None as a query value")
1446	            return lhs.get_lookup("isnull")(lhs, True)
1447	
1448	        # For Oracle '' is equivalent to null. The check must be done at this
1449	        # stage because join promotion can't be done in the compiler. Using
1450	        # DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS isn't nice but it's the best that can be done here.
1451	        # A similar thing is done in is_nullable(), too.
1452	        if (
1453	            lookup_name in ("exact", "iexact")
1454	            and lookup.rhs == ""
1455	            and connections[DEFAULT_DB_ALIAS].features.interprets_empty_strings_as_nulls
1456	        ):
1457	            return lhs.get_lookup("isnull")(lhs, True)
1458	
1459	        return lookup
1460	
1461	    def try_transform(self, lhs, name, lookups=None):
1462	        """
1463	        Helper method for build_lookup(). Try to fetch and initialize
1464	        a transform for name parameter from lhs.
1465	        """
1466	        transform_class = lhs.get_transform(name)
1467	        if transform_class:
1468	            return transform_class(lhs)
1469	        else:
1470	            output_field = lhs.output_field.__class__
1471	            suggested_lookups = difflib.get_close_matches(
1472	                name, lhs.output_field.get_lookups()
1473	            )
1474	            if suggested_lookups:
1475	                suggestion = ", perhaps you meant %s?" % " or ".join(suggested_lookups)
1476	            else:
1477	                suggestion = "."
1478	            if lookups is not None:
1479	                name_index = lookups.index(name)
1480	                unsupported_lookup = LOOKUP_SEP.join(lookups[name_index:])
1481	            else:
1482	                unsupported_lookup = name
1483	            raise FieldError(
1484	                "Unsupported lookup '%s' for %s or join on the field not "
1485	                "permitted%s" % (unsupported_lookup, output_field.__name__, suggestion)
1486	            )

1488	    def build_filter(
1489	        self,
1490	        filter_expr,
1491	        branch_negated=False,
1492	        current_negated=False,
1493	        can_reuse=None,
1494	        allow_joins=True,
1495	        split_subq=True,
1496	        check_filterable=True,

1502	        to this Query. Query.add_q() will then add this filter to the where

1505	        The 'branch_negated' tells us if the current branch contains any
1506	        negations. This will be used to determine if subqueries are needed.
1507	
1508	        The 'current_negated' is used to determine if the current filter is
1509	        negated or not and this will be used to determine if IS NULL filtering
1510	        is needed.
1511	
1512	        The difference between current_negated and branch_negated is that
1513	        branch_negated is set on first negation, but current_negated is
1514	        flipped for each negation.
1515	
1516	        Note that add_filter will not do any negating itself, that is done
1517	        upper in the code by add_q().
1518	
1519	        The 'can_reuse' is a set of reusable joins for multijoins.
1520	
1521	        The method will create a filter clause that can be added to the current
1522	        query. However, if the filter isn't added to the query then the caller
1523	        is responsible for unreffing the joins used.

1529	                filter_expr,
1530	                branch_negated=branch_negated,
1531	                current_negated=current_negated,
1532	                used_aliases=can_reuse,
1533	                allow_joins=allow_joins,
1534	                split_subq=split_subq,
1535	                check_filterable=check_filterable,
1536	                summarize=summarize,
1537	                update_join_types=update_join_types,
1538	            )
1539	        if hasattr(filter_expr, "resolve_expression"):
1540	            if not getattr(filter_expr, "conditional", False):
1541	                raise TypeError("Cannot filter against a non-conditional expression.")

1543	                self, allow_joins=allow_joins, reuse=can_reuse, summarize=summarize
1544	            )

1546	                condition = self.build_lookup(["exact"], condition, True)

1561	        used_joins = {
1562	            k for k, v in self.alias_refcount.items() if v > pre_joins.get(k, 0)
1563	        }
1564	
1565	        if check_filterable:

1567	
1568	        if reffed_expression:
1569	            condition = self.build_lookup(lookups, reffed_expression, value)
1570	            return WhereNode([condition], connector=AND), []
1571	
1572	        opts = self.get_meta()
1573	        alias = self.get_initial_alias()
1574	        allow_many = not branch_negated or not split_subq
1575	
1576	        try:
1577	            join_info = self.setup_joins(
1578	                parts,
1579	                opts,
1580	                alias,
1581	                can_reuse=can_reuse,
1582	                allow_many=allow_many,
1583	            )
1584	
1585	            # Prevent iterator from being consumed by check_related_objects()
1586	            if isinstance(value, Iterator):

1595	
1596	        # Update used_joins before trimming since they are reused to determine
1597	        # which joins could be later promoted to INNER.
1598	        used_joins.update(join_info.joins)
1599	        targets, alias, join_list = self.trim_joins(
1600	            join_info.targets, join_info.joins, join_info.path
1601	        )
1602	        if can_reuse is not None:
1603	            can_reuse.update(join_list)
1604	
1605	        if join_info.final_field.is_relation:
1606	            if len(targets) == 1:

1608	            else:
1609	                col = ColPairs(alias, targets, join_info.targets, join_info.final_field)
1610	        else:
1611	            col = self._get_col(targets[0], join_info.final_field, alias)
1612	
1613	        condition = self.build_lookup(lookups, col, value)
1614	        lookup_type = condition.lookup_name
1615	        clause = WhereNode([condition], connector=AND)

1617	        require_outer = (

1644	                        lookup_type == "in"

1646	                        and isinstance(condition.rhs, Iterable)
1647	                        and not isinstance(condition.rhs, (str, bytes))

1652	                        clause.add(lookup_class(col, False), AND)

1654	                # added.
1655	                if isinstance(value, Col) and self.is_nullable(value.target):

1663	    def add_q(self, q_object, reuse_all=False):
1664	        """
1665	        A preprocessor for the internal _add_q(). Responsible for doing final
1666	        join promotion.
1667	        """
1668	        # For join promotion this case is doing an AND for the added q_object
1669	        # and existing conditions. So, any existing inner join forces the join
1670	        # type to remain inner. Existing outer joins can however be demoted.
1671	        # (Consider case where rel_a is LOUTER and rel_a__col=1 is added - if
1672	        # rel_a doesn't produce any rows, then the whole condition must fail.
1673	        # So, demotion is OK.
1674	        existing_inner = {
1675	            a for a in self.alias_map if self.alias_map[a].join_type == INNER
1676	        }
1677	        if reuse_all:
1678	            can_reuse = set(self.alias_map)
1679	        else:
1680	            can_reuse = self.used_aliases
1681	        clause, _ = self._add_q(q_object, can_reuse)
1682	        if clause:
1683	            self.where.add(clause, AND)
1684	        self.demote_joins(existing_inner)
1685	
1686	    def build_where(self, filter_expr):
1687	        return self.build_filter(filter_expr, allow_joins=False)[0]
1688	
1689	    def clear_where(self):
1690	        self.where = WhereNode()

1692	    def _add_q(
1693	        self,
1694	        q_object,
1695	        used_aliases,
1696	        branch_negated=False,
1697	        current_negated=False,
1698	        allow_joins=True,
1699	        split_subq=True,
1700	        check_filterable=True,
1701	        summarize=False,
1702	        update_join_types=True,
1703	    ):
1704	        """Add a Q-object to the current filter."""
1705	        connector = q_object.connector
1706	        current_negated ^= q_object.negated
1707	        branch_negated = branch_negated or q_object.negated
1708	        target_clause = WhereNode(connector=connector, negated=q_object.negated)
1709	        joinpromoter = JoinPromoter(
1710	            q_object.connector, len(q_object.children), current_negated
1711	        )
1712	        for child in q_object.children:
1713	            child_clause, needed_inner = self.build_filter(
1714	                child,
1715	                can_reuse=used_aliases,
1716	                branch_negated=branch_negated,
1717	                current_negated=current_negated,
1718	                allow_joins=allow_joins,
1719	                split_subq=split_subq,
1720	                check_filterable=check_filterable,
1721	                summarize=summarize,
1722	                update_join_types=update_join_types,
1723	            )
1724	            joinpromoter.add_votes(needed_inner)
1725	            if child_clause:
1726	                target_clause.add(child_clause, connector)
1727	        if update_join_types:
1728	            needed_inner = joinpromoter.update_join_types(self)
1729	        else:
1730	            needed_inner = []
1731	        return target_clause, needed_inner
